title: Construction Coordinator
description: **Position Summary:** Arcadis Group seeks a Construction Coordinator (CC) to supervise and coordinate tool\-related activities, ensuring safe, reliable delivery of Master Schedule milestones. The CC partners with the PIT Lead and/or TIPM as an empowered PIT representative, leading at the point of work and collaborating with Trade Partners, Tool Owners, System Owners, and third parties. The CC monitors tool progress (e.g., Prefac, Move\-in \& Set, SL1\-C/SL2\-C), ensuring activities are well planned, coordinated, and executed to meet commitments. Acting as the first line of communication for field TI operations, the CC identifies and removes constraints, mitigates risks, escalates unresolved issues, and enforces procedural discipline to maintain a safe work environment. **Responsibilities include:** **Safety** * Ensure adherence to Work Control Processes and safe work practices; drive daily safety excellence. * Maintain housekeeping standards and proper handling, disposal, and transport of materials/equipment. * Identify and mitigate production interruption risks before Prefac through coordinated trade execution. * Conduct daily inspections, enforce safe conditions, stop work if needed, and perform PTP audits with proactive coaching. **Quality** * Support QCR/QAR inspections and milestone delivery; verify readiness and monitor quality. * Coordinate third\-party testing/certification (e.g., pressure, analytical, voltage, exhaust T\&B) and validate system integrity before testing. **Execution** * Coordinate vendors, including tool manufacturer dependencies, assembly, and logistics. * Manage Work Control processes (SIPP, eFIT, Tool Reservation). * Apply Last Planner® System practices (Pull Planning, Weekly Planning, Daily Huddles); manage field conditions, conflicts, and constraints. * Communicate short\-interval schedules aligned with PIT and project plans. * Oversee safe, quality move\-in/out of tools, pedestals, and equipment without production impact. * Lead and supervise move\-in/out activities, ensuring required documentation (SIPP, Work Plan, Risk Assessment). * Remove roadblocks, escalate issues, and enforce MIMO schedule adherence. * Supervise pedestal installation and inspections; escalate defects. * Maintain field presence for real\-time supervision and problem\-solving. * Ensure compliance with e\-Fit timelines and IDP guidelines; flag risks to production. * Develop and improve BKMs; drive IFF culture among contractors. * Collaborate with technical teams and Development PMs on implementation planning and rollout. **Education \& Experience:** * 5\+ years in semiconductor/high\-tech construction, or * 4\-year degree in construction/engineering \+ 8 years relevant experience, or * 2\-year technical degree \+ 5\+ years relevant experience. * Experience across the full development lifecycle from concept to delivery. **Essential Skills:** * Self\-motivated; able to prioritize, meet deadlines, and adapt to change * Flexible, independent, and team\-oriented * Strong analytical, problem\-solving, leadership, and communication skills * Results\-driven; thrives in fast\-paced environments * Excellent written and verbal English **Other:** * 40 hours/week, Monday–Friday * Site\-based role in Leixlip, Co. Kildare

title: Content Relevance Checker
description: Tech Pipeline helps companies resolve operational drag by fixing the technical gaps in their business processes. We focus on ground truth and the actual logs of how work moves through a system. We are looking for a Content Relevance Checker to join us in making sure our internal documentation and project summaries are accurate and consistent. This is a paid contract position with a fixed wage, and we are looking for someone currently based in Ireland to help us maintain our standards of technical clarity. **About the role** When Tech Pipeline audits a client system, we produce a high volume of technical drafts, operational summaries, and project reports. The accuracy of these documents is vital because a single error in a routing rule description or a misread log entry can result in significant confusion. You will be responsible for reviewing these internal documents to ensure they are factually consistent and supported by the original data we collect. This is an entry level role suited for someone who is exceptionally detail oriented and enjoys the process of verifying information against a source of truth. As a hybrid position, you will work from our Dublin office two days a week and remotely for the remainder of your contract hours. **What you will be doing** * Reviewing AI assisted drafts and summaries to ensure every claim matches the original source material. * Checking internal documents for factual consistency and identifying any statements that are unclear or unsupported. * Verifying project summaries against technical logs to ensure routing rules and integration gaps are described accurately. * Following detailed review instructions for every set of documentation to maintain a high quality bar. * Documenting recurring issues in the drafting process to assist our team in refining our reporting templates. * Escalating unclear cases where the technical data and the draft summary are in conflict. * Maintaining consistency across repeated review tasks so that all client facing reports meet the same standard. * Supporting internal QA and documentation workflows to help project teams stay organized. **What we need from you** * A high level of attention to detail and a preference for precise, accurate work. * The ability to write clearly and professionally without using unnecessary jargon. * Strong reading comprehension skills and the ability to spot subtle contradictions in technical text. * A methodical approach to your work and the discipline to follow complex guidelines closely. * Current residency in Ireland and the ability to commute to our Dublin office for the hybrid portion of the role. * Familiarity with standard document editing software and basic data organization. * A professional attitude toward repetitive verification work and a focus on long term consistency. **Helpful background** * Previous experience in a role that required technical writing or heavy proofreading. * Basic exposure to business operations, such as inventory management or order processing systems. * An interest in how technical information is documented and shared within an organization. **What the role offers** * A structured part\-time contract engagement with a fixed wage. * A hybrid work arrangement that provides a balance between the office and remote work. * Direct insight into how technical operations teams identify and solve business friction. * Experience working with professional QA and workflow documentation standards. * Mentorship from operators who value ground truth and technical honesty. * Opportunities to develop a professional eye for technical accuracy and clear communication. **Working at Tech Pipeline** We work differently than many other firms. We prioritize long form documentation over frequent meetings and we rely on clear, asynchronous updates to keep our projects moving. At Tech Pipeline, we value ground truth: the actual logs and the specific routing rules: over high level strategy. This means we spend our time looking at what is actually happening in a system rather than making assumptions. We are professional, conversational, and grounded in our approach. Our goal is always to leave a client with a system that works perfectly and is easy for them to manage. People who do well here are those who take pride in the details and enjoy the process of untangling complex information to make it useful for others.

title: Cover Driver
description: We have an exciting opportunity for you to join our team of Couriers. **Who We Are** FedEx is the largest express transportation company in the world, connecting more than 220 countries and territories. We work every moment to deliver the most important package of someone’s day. Our team of passionate people know their day\-to\-day work is invaluable in delivering outstanding experience to our customers. 530,000 team members strong, we are as diverse as the world we serve. We love what we do, and we do it well. Our reach is big, and so are our dreams. Join us and let's write our next chapter together. **Working hours** : 39 per week **What You Will Be Doing** * Representing FedEx in front of our customers, courteously picking up and delivering their packages * Driving vehicles in FedEx fleet on defined routes in various postcodes, making sure customers’ packages get to the right place, on time * Making sure vehicles are always operated safely and legally * Following all FedEx Health and Safety and other company policies and procedures **What Do You Bring With You** * Be a reliable team player * Be energetic and enthusiastic * Have a strong customer focus * Have good communication skills * Have an eye for detail to make sure paperwork is correct * Holding a valid driving license for type of vehicle assigned is essential and held for 2\+ years would be an advantage. * Adaptability to external driving conditions and circumstances is required * Commercial driving experience would be an advantage * Basic geographical knowledge **What Do We Offer** * Attractive compensation package * Training to get you started and on\-the\-job learning opportunities * Extensive learning resources to further develop your skills and knowledge * Tuition Assistance Program (\*applicable for FedEx positions with a permanent contract) * Employee Assistance Program for you and your family in difficult life situations * Employee reduced\-rate shipping * Great career opportunities * FedEx is one of the worlds most admired companies and trusted brands year after year Bring your ideas, individuality, and dreams to our global community. Feel good about where you work. Choose your career path \- we’re ready to invest in your development. Join FedEx. Diversity \& Inclusion is more than the workplace. It’s the cultures we celebrate, the dreams we inspire, and creating a global community where everyone is accepted. At FedEx, diversity and inclusion happens because we do it together. FedEx in the UK is Disability Confident ‘Committed’. FedEx was built on a philosophy that puts people first, one we take seriously. We are an equal opportunity employer and we are committed to a diverse and inclusive workforce in which we provide growth opportunities for all Our Company FedEx is one of the world's largest express transportation companies and has consistently been selected as one of the top 10 World’s Most Admired Companies by "Fortune" magazine. Every day FedEx delivers for its customers with transportation and business solutions, serving more than 220 countries and territories around the globe. We can serve this global network due to our outstanding team of FedEx team members, who are tasked with making every FedEx experience outstanding. Our Philosophy The People\-Service\-Profit philosophy (P\-S\-P) describes the principles that govern every FedEx decision, policy or activity. FedEx takes care of our people; they, in turn, deliver the impeccable service demanded by our customers, who reward us with the profitability necessary to secure our future. The essential element in making the People\-Service\-Profit philosophy such a positive force for the company is where we close the circle, and return these profits back into the business, and invest back in our people. Our success in the industry is attributed to our people. Through our P\-S\-P philosophy, we have a work environment that encourages team members to be innovative in delivering the highest possible quality of service to our customers. We care for their well\-being, and value their contributions to the company. Our Culture Our culture is important for many reasons, and we intentionally bring it to life through our behaviors, actions and activities in every part of the world. The FedEx culture and values have been a cornerstone of our success and growth since we began in the early 1970’s. While other companies can copy our systems, infrastructure and processes, our culture makes us unique and is often a differentiating factor as we compete and grow in today’s global marketplace.

title: Customer Assistant
description: Joining us as a Customer Assistant is a great opportunity, whether it’s your first job and you’re looking to gain some valuable experience, or you have experience with retail and you’re looking for a new challenge, this is the start of a new career with us **About The Role** You will work onsite and report into the Assistant or Store Manager and your role could be to help on the tills, advise our customers, or work in our back shop. You will also: * Help our customers and put them at ease; whether that’s listening to find out more about them, answering questions and giving great advice on our fantastic products, recommending items they hadn’t considered or simply offering a basket \- it's the little things that make a difference * Get to know our in\-store offers and schemes and promote them \- the more info we can give our customers, the better. * Help our stores run smoothly \- this could look like ensuring we have the right products displayed on the shop floor, providing a great service on our tills, or helping with our stock in in the stock rooms * Work with other members in the team to provide a great shopping experience, ensuring the store is always clean and presentable to provide a store everyone can be proud of **What You’ll Need To Have** Full training is provided for this role, and we are looking for people who * Excel in a varied environment, working at pace * Communicate and listen effectively * Enjoy working as part of a team, promoting a collaborative team dynamic * Know that the little things make the biggest difference for customer interactions * Desire to be a proud brand ambassador for Boots and help our customers find the best products for them **It would be great if you also have** * Experience providing customer care and delivering great customer service but this is not essential. **Our Benefits** * Pension membership (PRSA) * Partial paid maternity leave * Discretionary annual bonus * Generous employee discounts * Flexible benefits scheme We have a great range of benefits in addition to the above that offer flexibility to suit you \- find out more at **boots.jobs/ireland** . Exclusions may apply, eligible roles only. *Please note, any salary estimates given on third\-party sites are not provided or endorsed by Boots and may not be accurate.* **Why Boots** At Boots, we foster a working environment where consideration and inclusivity help everyone to be themselves and reach their full potential. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer, passionate about embracing the diversity of our colleagues and providing a positive and inclusive working environment for all. As the heart of everything we do at Boots, with you, we change for the better. **What's Next** If your application is successful, you'll be invited to an in\-store interview in the next 14 days. Where a role is advertised as full\-time, we are open to discussing part\-time and job share options during the application process. If you require additional support as part of the application and interview process, we are happy to provide **reasonable adjustments** to help you to be at your best.

title: Customer Assistant - Maynooth, Straffan Road
description: **Summary** From our Head Office to the shop floor and everywhere in between our primary goal is to create and deliver a fantastic customer journey for everyone who shops at Lidl. **Customer obsessed, high energy and love learning every day?** If that sounds like you, earn €460\+ per week as a Customer Assistant on our standard 30 hour contract. You’ll work across different shift times, on varied tasks and with different colleagues. What does a normal day\* as a Customer Assistant look like? (\*there’s no such thing as ‘normal’ in retail but we’ve tried our best!) As a Customer Assistant you are required to sell alcohol, therefore you must be 18 or over to work in our store. **Shift Patterns:** * Morning shift: Starting at 5am or 6am with your team, you’ll be getting our award\-winning products fully stocked on the shop floor and our delicious bakery products ready for our customers to enjoy by opening time so they can Go Full Lidl! * Middle shift: Starting mid\-morning or early afternoon, you’ll make sure our stores are spotlessly clean, well presented and our products remain fresh, all while helping customers with queries on the shop floor and working on tills to minimise till queues * Late shift: From your start time in the afternoon, you’ll make sure our customers experience is top class by serving them with a smile while on the tills, restocking missing items and getting the store ready for the next day **What You'll Do** * Interacting with the customer in a pleasant, friendly and helpful manner * Ensuring stock loss controls are followed in all areas of the store * Maintaining store and external cleanliness and hygiene standards * Following freshness and rotation principles * Ensuring all waste is managed correctly * Assisting in the stock count process **What You'll Need** * A can\-do attitude and excellent customer service skills * The willingness to go the extra mile for our customers * To be responsible and reliable * To enjoy working in a fast\-paced, varied environment * A good team player * Preferably, previous experience in a customer facing role but this is not essential provided you have the right attitude **What You'll Receive** We offer a competitive and transparent salary system, that ensures pay equality across all positions at Lidl * €15\.40 rising to €17\.90 per hour after 3 years (supplementary pay outlined below) * Unsocial hours worked (12am to 7am) * 20 days holidays per annum pro rata * Company pension * Genuine opportunities for career development * Individuals recognised as high performing will be invited to join our Leadership Academy as part of your on\-going development * Circle K and Private Health Insurance discounts available for all employees * Bike to Work Scheme * Mobile and broadband discounts with Three network * Maternity \& Paternity Leave top up, Marriage Leave, Employee Assistance Programme **Year 1** * Basic Rate €15\.40 * €19\.25 (Unsocial Hours) * €23\.10 (Overtime/Sundays) * €30\.80 (Bank Holiday) **Year 2** * Basic Rate €16\.20 * €20\.25 (Unsocial Hours) * €24\.30 (Overtime/Sundays) * €32\.40 (Bank Holiday) **Year 3** * Basic Rate €16\.90 * €21\.13 (Unsocial Hours) * €25\.35 (Overtime/Sundays) * €33\.80 (Bank Holiday) **Year 4** * Basic Rate €17\.90 * €22\.38 (Unsocial Hours) * €26\.85 (Overtime/Sundays) * €35\.80 (Bank Holiday) Lidl is an equal opportunities employer. If you have any particular requirements, we will ensure any reasonable accommodations are implemented as part of the recruitment and selection process. We value diversity and inclusion in our workplace and want to ensure that all applicants have the same opportunity regardless of gender, civil status, family status, sexual orientation, age, disability, race, religious belief, political opinion or membership of the traveller community.

title: Customer Success Executive
description: At **Alkimii** , we’re on a mission to transform how hospitality businesses manage their people and property operations. Our platform helps hotels run smoother operations, improve team communication and create better workplaces for their employees. As we continue to grow, we’re looking for a **Customer Success Executive** who enjoys helping people, solving problems and delivering outstanding service to our customers. This role is perfect for someone who thrives in a fast\-paced environment and wants to develop their career in a growing SaaS company serving the hospitality industry. **What you’ll be doing** As part of our Customer Success team, you’ll be the **first point of contact for our customers** , supporting hospitality teams as they use Alkimii to manage their daily operations. In this role you will: • Respond to customer queries via phone and email in a timely and friendly manner • Troubleshoot issues and guide customers through solutions using the Alkimii platform • Ensure every interaction reflects the high standards of service our customers expect • Work closely with the wider team to improve the customer experience • Help customers get the most value from the platform by understanding how they use it in their operations **What success looks like in your first months** • You feel confident supporting customers and navigating the Alkimii platform • Customers receive quick, helpful and friendly responses to their queries • You build strong relationships with customers and become a trusted point of contact • You contribute ideas to improve the support experience and internal processes **We think you’ll thrive in this role if you:** • Have previous experience in customer service or a customer\-facing role • Have experience working in hospitality or enjoy working with hospitality businesses • Are organised and comfortable managing multiple conversations and priorities • Communicate clearly and enjoy helping people solve problems • Are proactive, curious and eager to learn new technology **Requirements** • Strong computer and technical skills • Fluent written and spoken English • Excellent communication and attention to detail • Eligibility to work full\-time in Ireland **What you can expect from us** 💰Full time competitive salary 💲Clevercards \- Employee Benefits 🩺 Wellbeing programs, learning and growth opportunities 🧘 ♀️ Flexible working week, because we take seriously the productivity and wellbeing of our team 🚀 You will work on meaningful and challenging tasks in a relaxed yet supportive environment. 🙋 4,000 Employee Referral Program 🌅 Work in a beautiful building located on Skerries Harbour overlooking the pier! we get the best sunsets! 👯 Quarterly team meet\-ups \& annual gathering **Why join Alkimii?** We’re a team that cares deeply about our customers and about each other. Our culture is built on **kindness, curiosity and ownership** , and we’re always looking for ways to improve how we work and how we support hospitality teams around the world. If you enjoy solving problems, helping people and being part of a growing company where your ideas matter, we’d love to hear from you. *Before you apply, we feel it’s important that you know a bit more about us…* We: * Enjoy being up early enough to see the sunrise * Appreciate a good barista coffee * Discipline ourselves with morning workouts * Feed our curiosity with a good book * Don’t take ourselves too seriously * Constantly ask ourselves how we can make it easier for our customers * Love long walks on the beach (yes, really!) All sounding good? Check out what your new commute might look like: https://goo.gl/maps/cf5LPcJmCBBPYZFR8

title: Customer Support Specialist (12 Month Fixed Term)
description: **Summary** From our Head Office to the shop floor and everywhere in between our primary goal is to create and deliver a fantastic customer journey for everyone who shops at Lidl. With over 7,000 colleagues across our stores, warehouses and offices in Ireland and Northern Ireland we are a big and ambitious team. We know our people are our greatest asset and our success is down to the commitment and dedication of our team. As a key member of our **Customer Care Department** , you will be on the front line of our customer experience. You'll ensure every Lidl customer receives excellent support by tackling their issues and championing their needs. We need a **Customer Support Specialist** who is passionate about delivering an exceptional customer experience. You'll be responsible for managing a high volume of customer complaints and queries, using your problem\-solving skills to ensure every customer interaction is a positive one. **What You'll Do** * Be the voice of Lidl Ireland \& Northern Ireland, providing exceptional customer support across digital (WhatsApp, Facebook Messenger, Email) and traditional (Telephone) channels. * Navigate and manage high volumes of customer contacts, with a particular expertise in social media interactions. * Drive customer satisfaction by delivering prompt, accurate, and empathetic frontline support, resolving inquiries efficiently. * Meet and surpass performance targets by adhering to established KPIs for contact volume, quality, and handling time. * Champion customer needs, turning complaints and claims into positive outcomes for both the customer and the business. * Leverage internal systems and cross\-functional partnerships to provide comprehensive and accurate information to customers. * Maintain meticulous records of all customer interactions and data within our Salesforce CRM system. * Consistently go above and beyond to ensure every customer interaction exceeds their expectations and reinforces their positive experience with Lidl. **What You'll Need** * Entry\-level candidates with strong communication skills are welcome to apply. * A passion for people and customer care. You genuinely enjoy helping others and go the extra mile to provide a great experience. * Exceptional communication skills, both written and verbal, with a knack for making complex issues easy to understand. * A talent for negotiation and problem\-solving. You have experience resolving customer complaints efficiently and professionally. * The ability to thrive under pressure and meet targets in a fast\-paced environment. * Strong organisational and time\-management skills to handle multiple priorities. * Excellent administrative skills with a solid working knowledge of Google Workspace. * Flexibility to work evenings and weekends as required. **What You'll Receive** We offer a competitive and transparent salary system, that ensures pay equality across all positions at Lidl. * €33,000 per annum * 20 days holidays per annum * Private employee medical insurance * Company pension * Flexible start and finish times * Initial training and ongoing development from an experienced team member * Excellent opportunities for career progression * Dynamic work environment * Moderparking * On\-site gym n office facility with free and canteen * Hybrid working model \- up to 2 days remote working per week * Mobile and broadband discounts with Three network Lidl is an equal opportunities employer. If you have any particular requirements, we will ensure any reasonable accommodations are implemented as part of the recruitment and selection process. We value diversity and inclusion in our workplace and want to ensure that all applicants have the same opportunity regardless of gender, civil status, family status, sexual orientation, age, disability, race, religious belief, political opinion or membership of the traveller community.

title: Cyber Security Engineer
description: **IAM Security Engineer\- 6 Months\- (700\-730\)** Our client is a leading global financial institution undergoing significant investment in next generation trading, risk, and analytics platforms. The organisation is focused on modernising its technology landscape through scalable enterprise engineering, cloud\-native architecture, and high\-performance distributed systems. This will be a fully onsite role. **About the Position** We are looking for an Cyber Security Engineer to join on an initial 6 month contract, to drive innovation within the organisation. This role will suit someone who is an IAM expert and who has delivered large scale projects. The successful candidate will play a key role in the migration to the Cloud. **Key Responsibilities** • Work across development teams to understand and aid in the delivery of system requirements. • Work with large, complex data sets that meet functional/ non\-functional requirements. • Build and improve processes supporting system transformation, structures, quality controls, dependency, and workload management. • Define and build deployment pipelines that enable faster, better, data\-informed decision\-making within the business. **Experience/Requirements** * 10\+ years Information Security * Strong hands\-on experience with industry standard SSO technologies and protocols (OAuth, OpenID Connect, FIDO, SCIM, LDAP, SAML) * IAM experience * Cloud exposure **Daily Rate: 700\-730** Contact\- K.mcgovern@brightwater.ie or 01 592 7875 To view all live jobs with Brightwater and market insights, please visit our website; www.brightwater.ie

title: Data Centre Specialist (Ireland)
description: **Job Summary** This is a shift work position, the purpose for this role is to perform preventative, corrective, and emergency mechanical and electrical maintenance work on the Data Center critical equipment while maintaining 100% uptime. **Key Responsibilities** * While acting as DCS you are responsible for ensuring the successful operation, maintenance and repair of all critical equipment systems while maintaining 100% uptime on site. * Plan completion of planned \& reactive work orders, assigned to you, to achieve and maintain 100% compliance every month i.e. all work orders completed on or before due date. * Assist in the planning, supervision and handover of any project related changes or improvements within the facility. Ensure that the Facilities Operations Centre (FOC), FOC Library, Stores \& Workshop are clean and tidy at all times. Carry out routine housekeeping check of your area and follow a clean as you go approach to your work. * Work closely with management and the wider operational team on the review of equipment failures and preparation of detailed root cause analysis and action plan reports. * Provide input into and develop site specific procedures associated with Maintenance and operation of critical equipment: SOP’s, EOP’s, Drills etc. * Assist in technical issues, project management, vendor management and technical reporting on all critical equipment and computerised systems. * Escort and supervise/monitor work of third\-party engineers and sub\-contractors to ensure their compliance with the site standards when performing maintenance or relevant activities. * Operate equipment, monitor and respond to alarms and abnormal conditions in the FOC as required. Ensure that EPMS, BMS, and Fire System alarms are responded to with the highest priority. **Key Requirements** * Work shifts longer than eight hours in duration. * You must be flexible in your working patterns to support requirements dictated by the rhythm of the business \& operational demands. * Good working ability on Microsoft Office Products, in particular Excel, Word, Outlook and PowerPoint * Committed to the delivery of excellent customer service. **Education** Diploma in Electrical Engineering or Mechanical Engineering

title: Data Engineer - Join a New Quant Data Engineering Team
description: Would you be interested in relocating to Dublin to be part of a new Quant Data Engineering team? This is reputable trading firm who are seeking super strong Python coders to work on a range of exciting trading data projects and a variety of new technologies including AI problems. You'll be joining a new team therefore they're hiring people at various levels. They're also industry agnostic, you don't need to have experience within finance. The technical side of the role is the most important requirement. Who do you need to be? You're either: * Strong Python coder within Data Engineering * Coming from an outstanding educational background, and worked with large/complex data sets If this is you \- get in touch. Tech stack is open.

title: Deli Assistant - Part Time
description: **Job Description** **Deli Assistant \- Full Time \- SPAR CAPEL STREET , DUBLIN 1** Join our team at SPAR as a Deli Assistant and be a part of a dynamic and customer\-focused environment. As a Deli Assistant, you will play a key role in providing excellent service to our customers while maintaining a clean and organized deli area. **The Role** * Prepare and serve a variety of deli products such as sandwiches, salads, and hot food items * Assist customers in selecting deli items and provide information on products * Maintain cleanliness and hygiene standards in the deli area * Receive and store deli supplies and products * Work collaboratively with team members to ensure efficient deli operations If you have a passion for food, enjoy working in a fast\-paced environment, and have excellent customer service skills, we would love to have you on our team. Apply now and start your journey as a Deli Assistant at SPAR! **Job Requirements** * A passion for food. * Experience is an advantage but full training provided. * The ability to work as part of a team in fast\-paced environment.

title: Deli Assistant / General Kitchen Assistant
description: Deli \& Kitchen All\-Rounder We are currently seeking a **reliable, dependable, and hardworking** Deli \& Kitchen All\-Rounder to join our team in a busy food service environment. This role offers a Monday to Friday schedule only – no evening shifts, no weekend work, and a great work\-life balance finishing each day at 3pm. **Reliability is essential for this role.** We are a small team and need someone who is punctual, takes pride in their work, and can be counted on to turn up and get the job done every day. This is a hands\-on role suited to someone who is organised, efficient, and comfortable working in a fast\-paced environment. We are looking for a team player who can think on their feet, use their initiative, and is always willing to lend a hand wherever needed. As a small business, flexibility is important. No two days are exactly the same, and the successful candidate must be willing to assist in all areas of the operation when required. Key Responsibilities * Preparing and serving fresh deli food * Assisting with food preparation and presentation * Maintaining high standards of cleanliness and hygiene * Restocking displays and monitoring stock levels * Providing friendly and efficient customer service * Ensuring food safety procedures are followed at all times All\-Rounder Duties As we are a small team, you may occasionally be required to assist with duties outside the deli area to support the smooth running of the business. These duties may include: * Assisting with basic kitchen preparation and cooking * Washing dishes and operating the dishwasher * Cleaning and maintaining kitchen and food preparation areas * Restocking supplies and monitoring stock levels * Supporting colleagues during busy periods * Carrying out general cleaning and housekeeping duties as required A positive attitude and willingness to help wherever needed are essential. Requirements * **Fluent English is essential** * **Previous food preparation experience is required** * Excellent reliability and punctuality * Ability to work in a fast\-paced environment * Strong teamwork and communication skills * Good attention to detail and hygiene standards * Ability to use initiative and work independently when required Positions Available * **Full\-Time Position (Priority Hire):** Monday to Friday, 7:45am – 3:00pm * **Part\-Time Position:** Approximately 20 hours per week Additional Information * A minimum one\-day paid trial is required as part of the hiring process. What We Offer * Friendly and supportive working environment * Competitive hourly rate (depending on experience) * Employee discount * Store discount * On\-site parking **Job Types:** Full\-time, Part\-time **Work Location:** In person If you are interested in joining our team, please apply with your CV. We look forward to hearing from reliable and motivated candidates who are ready to become part of a small, hardworking team. Pay: €14\.15 per hour Benefits: * Employee discount * On\-site parking * Store discount Language: * English (required) Work Location: In person

title: Deli Team Member
description: **Deli Team Member \- Applegreen Portlaoise** As a Bakewell Team Member at Applegreen, you will be a crucial part of our front\-line operations. If you’re looking for a fulfilling role where you can make a difference every day and build lasting relationships, we’d love to hear from you! Apply now to join the Applegreen family and become part of a company that values community, teamwork, our customers. **What will I be doing as a Bakewell Team Member at Applegreen?** * Support day to day operations of the business. * Ensure shop floor is clean and tidy. * Ensure all food safety policies are met. * Food preparation. * Follow and enforce Bakewell manual training contents. * Stock control and management. * Create the best food experience possible for customers. * Work closely with management to achieve weekly and quarterly targets. **Why Should I join the Applegreen Team?** **Benefits** * All colleagues are eligible for a discount, allowing exclusive discounts on Bakewell Deli foods and hot drinks. * Bike to work scheme (available after 6 months of service). * HSF Health Plans schemes for healthcare expenses including dental, optical and many more. * Wellbeing platform with micro\-modules and articles to support your mental health and wellbeing. In addition, our Employee Assistance Programme is a free confidential counselling service which offers support on personal, family, work, and money matters. * Flexible schedules. * Company pension scheme. * Exclusive offers on broadband and mobile plans. * Refer a friend scheme. * Development opportunities through a variety of online and classroom\-based learning delivered by inhouse and external industry experts. **An Applegreen Bakewell Team Member would ideally:** * Have a can\-do attitude who has strong communication skills and enjoys interacting with customers. * Ability to work well with colleagues, contributing to a positive team environment. * Enjoy working in a fast\-paced environment. * Willingness to learn and adapt to new tasks. INDHP

title: Dev Ops Engineer
description: **About The Role** We’re looking for a **DevOps Engineer** to join our team at **The Dock** , where you’ll play a key role in shaping scalable, secure, and high\-performing engineering environments. You’ll work across **cloud infrastructure, CI/CD, automation, and observability** , helping teams deliver reliable, production\-ready solutions. This is not a pure operations role—you’ll contribute to engineering decisions, bringing a strong focus on **reliability, repeatability, and pragmatic delivery** . You’ll collaborate closely with **engineers, architects, data scientists, and product teams** , owning key technical components such as cloud environments, networking layers, repositories, and developer tooling—ensuring alignment with best practices and internal standards. This role suits someone with a **growth mindset** , who actively explores new technologies, shares knowledge, and communicates effectively with both technical and non\-technical stakeholders. **What You’ll Do** - Design, build, and maintain secure, scalable CI/CD pipelines (Azure DevOps, GitLab CI) - Develop and manage cloud\-native infrastructure using Infrastructure as Code - Deploy and operate containerised workloads (Docker, Kubernetes) - Implement monitoring, logging, tracing, and health\-check frameworks - Create reproducible environments to support consistency and engineering quality - Embed security across the lifecycle (DevSecOps) – secrets management, vulnerability scanning, dependency management - Manage network access and security across systems, including VPNs and firewalls, ensuring compliance with internal standards - Contribute to technical decision\-making, clearly communicating trade\-offs, risks, and improvements - Drive continuous improvement of development practices and code quality - Leverage AI tools to automate workflows, improve delivery, and support documentation - Write clean, maintainable, well\-tested code **What You Bring** - Hands\-on experience working in DevOps within agile delivery environments - Strong understanding of cloud platforms (Azure preferred; AWS/GCP also relevant) - Experience with Infrastructure as Code (e.g. Terraform, CloudFormation, Bicep/ARM) - Practical knowledge of containerisation and orchestration (Docker, Kubernetes, AKS/EKS/GKE) - Experience building and maintaining CI/CD pipelines and automation - Solid understanding of networking, distributed systems, and secure API design - Familiarity with monitoring, alerting, and logging frameworks - Ability to script in Python, Bash, or PowerShell - Understanding of authentication and authorisation concepts - Experience identifying and resolving security vulnerabilities (CVE) - Comfortable contributing to planning, estimation, and sprint delivery We welcome engineers from a variety of backgrounds who demonstrate **impact, ownership, and continuous learning** , rather than focusing on a fixed number of years of experience. **Nice to Have** - Experience leading workstreams or mentoring others - Relevant certifications (Azure, DevOps, Kubernetes) - Exposure to GenAI/LLM infrastructure (e.g. vector DBs, embeddings, orchestration) - Experience with secrets management (Vault, Azure Key Vault) and secure\-by\-design practices - Interest in system design, architecture, and modern software patterns

title: DevOps Engineer
description: We are partnering with a leading insurance client on a large\-scale transformation programme and are seeking a DevOps / QA Engineer to support the delivery of resilient CI/CD infrastructure, automated testing, and deployment reliability across critical enterprise platforms. This role sits between DevOps, platform engineering, and quality assurance teams, with a strong focus on automation, platform resilience, and continuous improvement. While not a formal management role, this position will suit someone who enjoys taking ownership, driving standards, and acting as a technical lead across CI/CD and QA initiatives. **Key Responsibilities** * Build, optimise, and maintain robust CI/CD infrastructure and deployment pipelines * Develop and maintain automated smoke, integration, performance, and security testing within the deployment lifecycle * Design and implement automated rollback and failover procedures to minimise downtime and improve platform resilience * Partner closely with engineering teams to identify build failures and drive technical remediation activities * Support continuous improvement across deployment automation, testing standards, and release quality * Prepare and deliver weekly system demonstrations covering infrastructure health, performance benchmarks, and security scanning metrics * Contribute to best practices across DevOps, QA automation, and release engineering processes **Required Experience** * 4\+ years’ experience across DevOps, CI/CD engineering, and QA/testing environments * Strong experience with CI/CD pipelines, deployment automation, and version control workflows * Hands\-on experience with automated testing frameworks for integration and infrastructure testing * Strong scripting capability with experience writing reusable automation and verification scripts * Experience troubleshooting deployment failures and supporting remediation activities across engineering teams * Strong communication skills with the ability to work cross\-functionally across technical teams **Desirable Experience** * Experience within enterprise or regulated environments * Exposure to cloud infrastructure and Infrastructure as Code practices * Experience supporting highly available production systems **How to Apply** If you are a DevOps / QA Engineer looking for a role with strong technical ownership please apply with your latest CV.

title: DevOps Engineer
description: **DevOps Engineer** **6 Months Contract** **Dublin\- 3 Days a Week at office is mandatory** Our client is a global technology company in the payments industry, operating in 210\+ countries and territories. Their mission is to power an inclusive, sustainable, digital economy that benefits everyone, everywhere — by making transactions safe, simple, smart, and accessible. **Key Experience:** * Strong working knowledge in DevOps tools (CI/CD pipelines in Jenkins), Git, Bitbucket, XLR * Good Linux skills (proper hands\-on of using commands and scripting) as most of the applications runs on file system. * Above average SQL skills. Should be able to write basic queries using SQLs using joins, sub\-queries, DMLs and DDLs. * Monitoring tools knowledge and experience in Splunk, Dynatrace or any tools * Good to have PROD Support experience (aware of ITSM Process and tools) – Workorder/Incident/Change/Problem management * Java based Application support/API support/Batch support

title: DevOps Engineer (€60k)
description: Some companies use DevOps to ship apps faster. This team is using it to help reshape how future medicines are discovered. We’re working with a fast\-growing deep\-tech company operating at the intersection of AI, computational science, and pharmaceutical innovation. Their platform is tackling one of the biggest challenges in drug development,, using advanced simulation and crystal structure prediction technology to accelerate research that traditionally takes years. Now they’re looking for a DevOps Engineer who loves building systems from the ground up. Not just maintaining infrastructure… but architecting the engine that powers cutting\-edge scientific computing. **This is the kind of role where you’ll:** ⚙️ Build self\-hosted CI/CD systems engineers actually enjoy using 📦 Orchestrate containerised workloads across distributed infrastructure 📊 Design observability stacks that give real\-time visibility into complex systems 🛠 Own infrastructure\-as\-code and automation from day one 🧠 Work directly with highly technical R\&D teams and leadership on scaling architecture 🚀 Help create an environment where experimentation and innovation can move faster, safely They’re not looking for someone who waits for tickets to appear. They want someone curious, hands\-on, and excited by solving hard infrastructure problems in a highly technical environment. **What you’ll likely bring:** • Strong experience in DevOps / Platform Engineering / SRE • Confidence working with Docker, Podman, or container ecosystems • Experience building CI/CD pipelines using GitHub Actions, GitLab CI/CD, Jenkins, or similar • Hands\-on knowledge of observability and monitoring stacks (Prometheus, Grafana, alerting systems, etc.) • Infrastructure\-as\-Code experience with tools like Ansible • Strong Linux fundamentals and scripting skills • Python proficiency for automation and tooling • A self\-starter mindset with the ability to work closely with technical and non\-technical stakeholders alike **Why this role stands out:** This isn’t another “keep the lights on” DevOps role. You’ll be joining a highly innovative, award\-winning environment where infrastructure directly supports scientific breakthroughs, AI\-driven research, and next\-generation pharmaceutical technology. If you enjoy autonomy, technical depth, and building scalable systems that actually matter, this is worth a conversation.

title: Device and Product Performance Scientist
description: McDermott Laboratories Limited Viatris is a global healthcare company uniquely positioned to bridge the traditional divide between generics and brands, combining the best of both to more holistically address healthcare needs globally. With a mission to empower people worldwide to live healthier at every stage of life, we provide access at scale, currently supplying high\-quality medicines to approximately 1 billion patients around the world annually and touching all of life's moments, from birth to the end of life, acute conditions to chronic diseases. We have been included on number of award lists that demonstrate the impact we are making. Our global portfolio includes best\-in\-class, iconic brand\-name products as well as global key brands; generics, including branded and complex generics; and other offerings across a wide range of therapeutic areas. We are committed to helping create healthier communities worldwide through education, outreach and better access to treatment. For those driven to live their lives with purpose, Viatris helps employees realise their full potential by creating a collaborative culture that fosters courage and resilience. Viatris is more than just a place to work. This is a place to make a difference in the world. **The Role \& What You Will Be Doing** Lead the development of combination Product methods R\&D Laboratory whilst providing technical mentorship to more junior members of the team, including guidance for technical issues and review/approval of documents as well as support for the lab manager. Every day, we rise to the challenge to make a difference and here’s how the **Device and Product Performance Scientist** role will make an impact: * Provision of technical laboratory expertise to support the development of Combination Products. * Provide technical support to a team responsible for activities such as metrology testing, engineering verification, design verification, device functional stability, biological evaluation, method development, transport \& shipping studies. * Provide technical mentorship to more junior members of the team, including guidance for technical issues and review/approval of documents. * Leading the Development and Validation of Test Methods to support Design Verification Testing either to internal/external test facilities or manufacturing sites. * Dealing with Viatris Affiliates and/or Third Partner Companies to support exhibit and registration batches, through review and implementation of controls and additional requirements if needed such as manufacturing investigations. * Responsible for testing of combination products within the Global Device Development portfolio including generation of protocols, oversight of execution of testing at external test facilities and writing of reports. * Trending and statistical analysis of analytical data including compilation of technical reports to support combination product development lifecycle. * Ensuring that the Combination Product meets any requirements for reliability, through implementation of appropriate controls and assessment of the product against suitable specifications. * Responsible for compilation and review of technical documentation within GDD in line with global regulatory requirements, guidance’s and the latest regulatory and industry thinking. * Lead and review device investigations and participate in GDD and technical investigation teams as required. Reporting and logging of aberrant results using Trackwise™. * Support of all R\&D Lab activities including, Equipment calibration and Qualification, Documentation requirements, Trackwise records. * Attending EHS training and maintaining responsibility for adhering to appropriate EHS procedures. Ensure the appropriate risk assessments are in place to perform required tasks. **About Your Skills \& Experience** For this role, we’re looking for a candidate who has an effective combination of the following qualifications, skills and experiences: * Experience in the development and validation of analytical methods is an advantage. * Experienced in application of statistical techniques for data analysis. * Masters/Degree in Engineering/Science with minimum 2 years’ experience in a lab environment. * Previous experience using in a regulated GMP environment would be an advantage * Working proficiency with Minitab would be an advantage. * Highly driven and motivated person who can work as part of a flexible, dynamic team whilst taking responsibility for individual tasks. * Working knowledge of design control requirements in line with FDA 21 CFR part 820 and ISO 13485 would be an advantage. * Well\-developed communication skills to lead or facilitate effective discussions. Able to communicate technical and/or project information to key stakeholders. * Some travel may be required as part of this role. **Benefits at Viatris** At Viatris, we offer competitive salaries, benefits and an inclusive environment where you can use your experiences, perspectives and skills to help make an impact on the lives of others. Some of our benefits include the following on top of a competitive salary: * Excellent career progression opportunities * Work\-life balance initiatives * Bonus scheme * Health insurance * Pension **Diversity \& Inclusion at Viatris** At Viatris, diversity and inclusion are essential to our mission. The diversity we foster in all aspects of our business can be one of our greatest strengths in redefining healthcare not as it is, but as it should be. If you would like to know more about what diversity, equity and inclusion means to us, please visit https://www.viatris.com/en/Careers/Diversity\-and\-Inclusion **Sustainability at Viatris** Corporate social responsibility is fundamental to the Viatris mission. We work to advance responsible and sustainable operations and leverage our collective expertise to empower people to live healthier at every stage of life, recognizing that our actions affect the stakeholders and communities we serve. To learn more about our efforts, please visit https://www.viatris.com/en/about\-us/corporate\-responsibility At Viatris, we offer competitive salaries, benefits and an inclusive environment where you can use your experiences, perspectives and skills to help make an impact on the lives of others. Viatris is an Equal Opportunity Employer.

title: Driver /RCO
description: **The Role** The Driver/Record Centre Operative is responsible for the responsibility for driving deliveries and collections whilst also being responsible for daily activity in the record centre, utilising software to control movement at the box, file tape and miscellaneous levels. The role is based at our site in Ballycoolin, Republic of Ireland. **Key Responsibilities** * Ensure all daily client work orders are filled upon receipt from Client Service. * Complete delivery runs as assigned by supervisor and drive and park assigned vehicles in a safe and legal manner at all times. * Ensure Rush work orders are delivered in a timely manner. * Refile files, containers and tapes daily and within the allocated timescales. * Comply with daily maintenance and safety checks of all record centre vehicles and equipment. * Help unload/load trucks/vans. * Operate forklifts and /or pick lifts. * Complete daily paperwork before leaving for the day * Verify and load all orders per SOP’s. * Complete daily safety inspection of assigned vehicle and report and defects immediately to Supervisor/Team Leader. * Comply with all company policies and all relevant data protection legislation to ensure. * Our clients and team members’ sensitive and personal information is protected and processed in accordance with the law. * Our team members’ and clients’ rights under the legislation are fully protected.. * Adhere to all company policies and complete all mandatory training modules. * Report any potential or actual security, environmental or health and safety incidents to your line manager and/ or the OASIS Compliance team as soon as you become aware of them. * Any other additional duties as deemed necessary to support the business. **Candidate Requirements** * Previous experience of working in a Multi Drop operation service delivery role preferably within the records management industry. * Full Clean Driving Licence. * Strong attention to detail essential. * Flexibility to work between teams. * Routine lifting. * Routine scanning of materials * Overhead reaching. * Bending and stretching. * Routine Pushing and pulling boxes and pallet jacks. * Ability to operate forklift and/or pick lift.

title: E-Commerce Specialist
description: Position As our **E\-Commerce Specialist,** you will play a pivotal role in driving the growth and optimising our digital presence across both our strategic third\-party business partners and across Europe E\-Commerce (The UK, Germany, Italy, France, and Spain) and International E\-Commerce. Reporting to the E\-Commerce Associate Manager, you will be responsible for maximizing sales performance, enhancing brand representation, and ensuring a seamless customer journey across all assigned digital channels. Job responsibilities **Business Partner Relationship \& Performance Management** * Strategic Relationship Management:Serve as a key operational and commercial point of contact for strategic e\-commerce business partners. Drive commercial activities and foster strong relationships to achieve sales targets and maximize brand visibility and representation on their platforms. * Process Enhancement:Develop, refine, and implement efficient processes for managing product launches and day\-to\-day operations with business partners to streamline workflows and improve efficiency. * External Auditing \& Quality Assurance:Consistently audit third\-party websites to ensure brand guidelines, content accuracy, and optimal user experience. Proactively identify and resolve any anomalies or issues, escalating to IT Teams or partners as necessary. * Buying \& Orders Support: Actively support the buying and order processes for business partners to ensure accurate stock levels, optimal product availability, and a relevant, market\-appropriate assortment that aligns with commercial objectives. **E\-commerce Operations \& Visual Merchandising** * Comprehensive Product Catalogue Stewardship: Oversee the end\-to\-end product catalogue presence across all business partners platforms. This includes meticulous management of new product uploads, ensuring absolute accuracy of all product information (imagery, pricing, descriptions), and verifying that all required assets are present and optimized. * Dynamic Stock Management: Implement and maintain accurate stock alignment across all digital channels, proactively managing product availability and ensuring the timely removal of out of strategy creations. * Digital Visual Merchandising: Execute sophisticated digital merchandising strategies to ensure products are optimally displayed across all front ends. Strategically highlight seasonal pieces, novelties, and key collections, in strict alignment with brand guidelines and visual merchandising objectives. * Content Localization \& Coherence: Review and ensure the consistent quality of content on Business Partner platforms, guaranteeing brand coherence, local relevance, and factual accuracy. **Performance Monitoring \& Optimisation** * Daily Performance Oversight:Conduct daily monitoring of website performance across all managed channels (direct, Crossborder and business partners) to identify opportunities for improvement in KPIs and sales trends., while contributing to the continuous enhancement of user experience (UX) across all platforms to drive conversion. * Growth Initiatives:Propose and influence plans for future EU and International E\-Commerce growth across all managed channels, contributing to strategic discussions and initiatives. **Reporting \& Analysis** * Data\-Driven Insights:Monitor, analyse, and report on sales trends, traffic, and key performance indicators (e.g., conversion rates, engagement, drop\-off rates) across all digital channels (direct and business partners). Translate data into actionable insights to optimize action plans and conversion rates for respective channels. * Market \& Competitor Analysis:Research and analyse business trends, conduct competitive benchmarking, and analyse customer behaviour data and activities across all managed channels to identify opportunities and best practices. Profile **Cross\-Functional Alignment** **Teams Collaboration:** Align with internal and external teams (e.g., Merchandising, Marketing, Sales, IT, Supply) to ensure coordination of e\-commerce activities and seamless execution across all direct and partner channels **Required Skills** * Bachelor’s degree in business, Marketing, E\-commerce, or a related field. * 2\+ years of E\-commerce experience, preferably managing both e\-commerce and third parties’ platforms. * Advanced knowledge and practical experience with web analytics tools, especially Google Analytics. * High proficiency in Microsoft Excel and Power BI for in\-depth data analysis, manipulation, and reporting. * Prior experience within the fashion, luxury, retail, or lifestyle sectors is highly preferred. * Proficiency in Salesforce Commerce Cloud (SFCC) is a plus. Additional information **Competences** * A highly self\-motivated and driven individual who takes initiative, manages multiple priorities effectively, and operates with a keen sense of ownership and accountability. * Strong analytical thinking with the ability to dissect data and translate insights into actionable strategies. * A proactive and solutions\-oriented mindset. * Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to build strong relationships, foster collaboration, and work effectively as a team player. * Meticulous attention to detail, ensuring accuracy and precision in all tasks.

title: EMEA Payroll Analyst
description: *To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.* Job Category Finance Job Details **About Salesforce** Salesforce is the \#1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all. Ready to level\-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce. Salesforce is currently seeking a Payroll Analyst to join our well established EMEA Payroll team and to support our continuous company growth. This individual will be responsible for processing our payroll, as well as taking part in exciting projects which require cross\-functional collaboration with external and internal partners. The successful candidate will have 3\+ years of experience processing payroll for large volumes of employees (ideally 1000\+). **Key Responsibilities:** * Manage end\-to\-end payroll processing for employees (EMEA payrolls) using our external providers, including salary calculations, deductions, bonuses, and overtime payments. * Deliver monthly and annual tax filings, social security contributions, and statutory filings. * Perform accurate monthly SOX controls for the payroll. * Maintain accurate payroll records, employee data, tax information, and ensure updates are processed promptly in the payroll system. * Prepare payroll reports as required, assist our accounting team with payroll related queries and support internal and external audits related to payroll. * Address payroll\-related inquiries from employees, resolving discrepancies and providing clear explanations of pay and deductions. * Identify areas for process improvement, system enhancements, and automation to streamline payroll operations. * Collaborate with our stakeholder teams across the business to ensure alignment on compensation, benefits, and financial reporting. **Requirements:** * Minimum 3 years of experience working in a payroll processing role, demonstrating a strong understanding of payroll compliance. * Proficient in using payroll systems (e.g., SAP, ADP, or similar) and Microsoft Excel for data analysis and reporting. * High level of accuracy and detail orientation in payroll calculations and compliance. * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills to resolve payroll discrepancies and implement process improvements. * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to explain complex payroll concepts to non\-payroll staff * Fluent in English Unleash Your Potential When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and *be your best* , and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can *do your best* . Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world. Humans will always make any candidate selection and hiring decisions. It means that at Salesforce, we believe in equality for all. And we believe we can lead the path to equality in part by creating a workplace that’s inclusive, and free from discrimination. Any employee or potential employee will be assessed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications – without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender expression or identity, transgender status, age, disability, veteran or marital status, political viewpoint, or other classifications protected by law. title: Engineer, Cloud Operations
description: **Job Overview** The NOC Engineer will be responsible for monitoring, maintaining, and operating applications, batch processes, network, and infrastructure components. This hands\-on technical role ensures high availability (99\.9%\-99\.99%) and supports timely resolution of incidents or technical escalations to meet established SLAs. The position focuses on delivering operational excellence and reliability within automated cloud environments that power iCIMS products. **The Network Operations Center (NOC), a key part of iCIMS Technical Operations, is dedicated to monitoring applications and infrastructure to deliver an exceptional customer experience. The team ensures optimal performance by validating availability, coordinating cross\-functional event responses, and communicating any customer\-impacting incidents. Additionally, the NOC analyzes key performance indicators (KPIs) to forecast future trends and provide initial recommendations to the engineering team.** **The Engineer, NOC reports to the NOC Manager, will be responsible for maintaining the functionality of applications, batch processes, network, and infrastructure components. This role ensures maximum availability (99\.9%\-99\.99%) and drives timely resolution of incidents or technical escalations to meet established SLAs.** **About Us** When you join iCIMS, you join the team helping global companies transform business and the world through the power of talent. Our customers do amazing things: design rocket ships, create vaccines, deliver consumer goods globally, overnight, with a smile. As the Talent Cloud company, we empower these organizations to attract, engage, hire, and advance the right talent. We’re passionate about helping companies build a diverse, winning workforce and about building our home team. We're dedicated to fostering an inclusive, purpose\-driven, and innovative work environment where everyone belongs **Responsibilities** * Ensure Production Stability: Monitor availability and performance across the entire production environment to maintain optimal operations. * Leverage Monitoring Tools: Track cloud resource utilization and performance metrics to identify trends and potential issues proactively. * Data\-Driven Insights: Generate regular performance reports and recommend enhancements based on detailed analysis. * Incident Management Excellence: Lead the restoration of normal service operations swiftly, including assessment, research, escalation, communication, and resolution management. * Execute Production Changes: Implement necessary changes to support both internal and external customer needs * Operational Support: Provide effective triage and resolution for operational support requests. * Documentation \& Standards: Review and refine SOPs, policies, procedures, and system requirements to ensure accuracy and relevance. * Automation Development: Create and maintain automation scripts using Python and Java to streamline processes and reduce manual effort. * Infrastructure as Code (IaC): Apply IaC practices to improve deployment efficiency, consistency, and scalability. * Comprehensive Documentation: Prepare detailed electronic documentation, including SLAs, performance metrics, installation guides, and implementation guides. * Reduce Manual Work: Identify repetitive tasks and implement automation solutions to eliminate inefficiencies. * Performance Reviews: Participate in monthly metric reviews to support uptime goals of 99\.9%\-99\.99%. * Drive Innovation: Demonstrate passion, initiative, and urgency in seeking innovative solutions and resolving issues effectively. **Qualifications** **Technical Expertise** * 2 to 4 years in administration and production support experience with on\-call responsibilities * 1\+ years of strong Cloud provider experience and demonstrated knowledge * Observability tooling experience **Preferred** **Preferred Qualifications** * Experience with AWS / AWS Certifications * Exposure to other cloud technologies like Azure and GCP **EEO Statement** iCIMS is a place where everyone belongs. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. Our approach helps us to build a winning team that represents a variety of backgrounds, perspectives, and abilities. So, regardless of how your diversity expresses itself, you can find a home here at iCIMS.
