title: Graduate Building Surveyor
description: Graduate / APC Level Building Surveyor \- North Dublin Our client is a leading, multi\-disciplinary practice providing premium residential and commercial property solutions across Dublin and the surrounding counties. Operating from a North Dublin office they have a team of experienced Surveyors. They can provide a structured, supportive, and practical environment designed to help early\-career surveyors rapidly develop their skills and successfully achieve Chartership. **Role Overview** They are seeking an ambitious and enthusiastic **Graduate through to APC\-Level Building Surveyors** to join our growing North Dublin team. In this role, you will gain hands\-on, diverse experience across both the residential and commercial sectors. Working directly alongside Directors and Senior Chartered Surveyors, you will be fully supported through the **SCSI / RICS Assessment of Professional Competence (APC)** pathway, with access to diverse project work and structured mentorship to help you pass your diary and final assessment. * **Location:** North Dublin with site travel. * **Salary:** Competitive (Commensurate with experience) \+ APC support package. * **Reporting to:** Director **Key Responsibilities** In tis role you will assist with and gradually take ownership of a broad range of building surveying instructions, including: **Professional \& Technical Services** * Carrying out residential Pre\-Purchase House Surveys and Homebuyer Surveys. * Conducting detailed Snag Inspections and compiling comprehensive Snag List Reports for new\-build residential developments. * Assisting with Commercial Building Surveys, Schedule of Conditions, and Dilapidations assessments. * Conducting measured building surveys using advanced electronic surveying/scanning software to generate accurate real\-time floor plans. **Contract Administration \& Design** * Assisting with the design, specification, and contract administration of refurbishment, repair, and planned maintenance projects. * Contributing to Planning Applications and building control compliance tracking. * Preparing Insurance Rebuilding and Reinstatement Cost Assessments (BCIS/SCSI guidelines). * Undertaking Land Registry and boundary mapping surveys. **Client \& Progress Management** * Drafting high\-quality, technically accurate professional reports for private individuals, corporate clients, financial institutions, and solicitors. * Managing client communications professionally, responding to enquiries, and updating the internal tracking systems. **Required Qualifications \& Experience** * **Education:** A BSc (Hons) degree in Building Surveying (ideally from an SCSI/RICS accredited course). * **Experience:** Open to recent graduates, as well as those with 1–3 years of post\-graduate experience actively working through their APC diary. * **Technical Aptitude:** Strong IT literacy, including familiarity with AutoCAD and modern digital surveying equipment/apps. * **Knowledge:** A solid foundation in construction technology, building pathology, and Irish Building Regulations. * **Driving License:** A full, clean Irish/EU driving license and access to a car is **essential** for site visits across Dublin and surrounding counties. **Key Competencies** **Commitment to Learning:** A clear drive to pursue and complete the APC process to become a Chartered Building Surveyor. **Attention to Detail:** An analytical eye for spotting building defects, poor workmanship, and structural anomalies. **Good Communication Skills:** Excellent verbal and written communication skills to write robust technical reports and communicate clearly with clients. **On Offer** * **Full APC Structured Support:** Dedicated mentorship from registered SCSI/RICS supervisors and counselors, structured diary rotation, and mock interview preparation. * **Diverse Case Load:** Exposure to both residential and commercial projects, ensuring a well\-rounded foundation for your career and your APC submissions. * **Professional Fees Paid:** SCSI/RICS professional membership and subscription fees covered. * **Progression:** A clear pathway to a Post\-Chartered Surveyor role within the firm upon successful completion of your APC. * **Culture:** A collaborative, professional, and friendly working environment in North Dublin with free parking on site. If you are a building surveyor looking for your next challenge then this could be a good role for you. Please share your latest CV to rhughes@macdonaldandcompany.com

title: Graduate CSA Engineer
description: Graduate CSA Engineer, Ballycoolin Business Park, Dublin Apply Now **Mercury is the European leader in construction solutions.** We build and manage complex engineering \& construction projects for the world’s leading corporations. Our solutions help deliver technologies and life changing advancements that connect people, communities and businesses, giving them the power to achieve incredible things. Our people have the courage to be innovative. Their determination and sharp focus enable us to deliver with certainty, time and time again. We deliver our clients’ visions through leading edge construction solutions across multiple sectors, including Enterprise Data Centres, Advanced Technology \& Life Sciences, Hyperscale Data Centres and Fire Protection. At Mercury, it is our duty to encourage and back our people to realise their vision of themselves. We place them at the heart of what we do, providing challenging opportunities to develop within a great team in a supportive environment that allows them to reach their full potential. **Our Graduate Programme** We’re seeking the leaders of tomorrow to join us on our Graduate Programme \- those who are willing to share our values, live our culture and deliver beyond expectations. At Mercury we know that to achieve incredible things you must invest in your people. Our dedication to shaping the leaders of tomorrow shines through our highly regarded graduate programme and ensures we stay ahead in a competitive market. From day one you will be on a stellar career trajectory with no limits to what you can achieve, and the programme empowers you to develop the essential skills needed to excel in the fast\-paced construction industry. Since launching in 2009, we have successfully trained 360\+ graduates, many of whom have forged long\-lasting careers with us. Our comprehensive eighteen\-month programme is tailored to meet real\-world demands, featuring courses led by both external and internal subject matter experts in a focused learning environment. Graduates on our programme can anticipate working alongside a diverse and supportive team, accessing state\-of\-the\-art training and masterclasses, and gaining international experience at the leading edge of industry. An important aspect of the development programme is the completion of a group research project. Graduates are split into small groups of four or five and assigned a strategic business project to research and present their findings to Senior Management. All topics are chosen by Senior Management and are relevant to Mercury's business. **Roles\& Responnsibilities** * Management of the CSA package. * Management of Site Safety in conjunction with the project manager and the Mercury Safety Advisor. This will include the setup, management of and closing out of the safety file as part of the Management team. * Management of Site QA with regards to the CSA works on site ensuring that a robust system is in place to avoid future issues. Record and documents all facets of the works. * Management of Project Schedule in conjunction with Mercury Project manager, consulting Engineer and Client requirements * Management of all day to day site operations * Ensure Mercury Policies and procedures are followed closely. * Management of sub\-contractor activities including tracking progress against schedule and manpower levels. Ensure that there is, and you attend, a weekly minuted meeting with sub\-contractor. * Management of the Mercury “In Progress” snagging system ensuring that the supervisors return weekly punch lists where applicable. Ensure all snags are distributed to all Sub\-contractors * Issue direct labour hours and sub\-contractor hours to time administrator for tracking centrally. * Attendance at Site Meetings, Internal Project Reviews, Engineering Reviews, Planning and Co\-ordination Meetings and Handover Meetings * Close out of all project items including O \& M Manuals, Punch List, Safety File, QA File, Project Plan – all with the support of the site project manager and your project team. * Issue internal weekly progress report, to the project manager, highlighting areas not available, delays, shortage of design, third party hold ups and percentage complete. * Maintain a weekly project photographic diary * All other duties associated with this role and/or reasonably required by the Company the Company reserves the right to vary the scope and nature of the Employee’s normal duties to take into account changes in the Company’s work patterns and practices and the requirements of its business. **Essential** **Selection Criteria / Person Specification:** * Ideally 2:1 Honours Degree / Expected 2:1 * Level 8 or relevant bachelor’s degree * Good communication skills * Strong interpersonal and organisational skills * Adaptable and solutions driven * Able to work in a team environment * Pride in the quality and presentation of work * Good organization \& coordination skills * Flexible to travel * Enthusiastic and energetic **Desirable** * Experience in the Construction Industry or Trade Background Apply Now

title: Graduate Ecologist - Dublin, IRE - June 2026 start onwards
description: Work with Us. Change the World. At AECOM, we're delivering a better world. Whether improving your commute, keeping the lights on, providing access to clean water, or transforming skylines, our work helps people and communities thrive. We are the world's trusted infrastructure consulting firm, partnering with clients to solve the world’s most complex challenges and build legacies for future generations. There has never been a better time to be at AECOM. With accelerating infrastructure investment worldwide, our services are in great demand. We invite you to bring your bold ideas and big dreams and become part of a global team of nearly 50,000 planners, designers, engineers, scientists, digital innovators, program and construction managers and other professionals delivering projects that create a positive and tangible impact around the world. We're one global team driven by our common purpose to deliver a better world. Join us. **Job Description** AECOM are seeking a Graduate Ecologist in our Dublin Office to join us from June 2026\. This is a fantastic opportunity to further your career by gaining valuable work experience within an established and successful consultancy, supporting the growing ecology team in Ireland. **About Our Team** Ecology is part of AECOM’s Environment business (one of the leading environmental consultancies in the UK and Ireland) which delivers environmental management services for feasibility, impact analysis and operational compliance as well as remediation, restoration and re\-use of impacted property. More than160 professional ecologists support AECOM’s goal to deliver a better world through effective management of the biodiversity that underpins natural capital. We work across terrestrial, freshwater and marine environments in a wide range of sectors: power; utilities; development; government; manufacturing; oil and gas; and transportation. Most of our work is based in Ireland and the UK, and there may also be opportunities overseas including Europe, Middle East and Africa. Our ecologists work alongside architects, engineers, planners, economists and other environmental specialists on important projects. Together we provide the full range of ecological services including habitat and species surveys, impact assessment, advice on biodiversity mitigation, offsetting and enhancement, and management planning for biodiversity. **Here's what you'll do:** In this role you will be working within a multi\-disciplinary team on field surveys including preliminary ecological appraisal, Phase 1 habitat surveys, other botanical surveys, ecological impact assessment and a range of protected species surveys. From time to time, fieldwork is likely to require working away from home and some working at night when surveying. In addition to field skills, good organization, research and planning skills are also required to support a team which works safely and to a high technical standard. You’ll also need to be able to work with a range of taxonomic groups, and you’ll be encouraged to pursue your own interests. **Enjoy the Perks** At AECOM, you'll enjoy a range of core and personalised benefits designed to support your future and well\-being, customised to fit your lifestyle. Take advantage of career development opportunities, our flexible hybrid working model to ensure a work\-life balance that suits your lifestyle, technical practice networks, AECOM University, and volunteering days. **Here is what you will get from us!** On the job support and learning When a graduate starts at AECOM, we pair them with a senior member of the team who will guide and mentor them through their initial period working on live projects and business development tasks, to support with any technical queries. As an Ecology Graduate with AECOM, you will be trained in technical skills, safety, project management and business systems. We’ll support you with a structured graduate development programme to help you realise your full potential as an ecologist on some of the most significant projects in Ireland, the UK and overseas. Graduate Development Programme Our 2\-year graduate ADVANCE programme is designed to help you build your career in AECOM. You will attend several instructor\-led training sessions where you will meet and hear from people across the business and take part in a series of interactive activities to equip you with the knowledge and skills you need to succeed in your role. Alongside the instructor\-led elements of the ADVANCE programme, you will also engage with an online curriculum via AECOM University and can join the ADVANCE webinar series. We will provide you with all the support and tools you need to achieve your professional development ambitions, which may include Chartership with the relevant international professional institution. We have more than 750 graduates on our programmes across Ireland and the UK from more than 40 different disciplines, each making a tangible difference to the environment in which we live and work. The Professional Institute you will work towards is the Chartered Institute for Ecologists and Environmental Managers (CIEEM). See our digital early careers e\-brochure here **Qualifications** * 2:1 degree and above (or equivalent) in Ecology or a subject with ecological component \- Ecology, Environmental Biology, Zoology, Environmental Diagnosis and Management, Wildlife Conservation/Biodiversity, Conservation Biology and Resource Management, Species Identification and Surveying, Environmental Science/Consultancy, Environmental Management and Sustainability, Biological Science * Keen interest and understanding of Irish wildlife * Irish plant and animal (indirect and direct field signs) identification skills * Excellent field skills * Ability to record data efficiently and methodically * Strong Microsoft Office skills including Microsoft Word and Excel * Strong numeracy * Ability to communicate effectively to internal and external clients, both verbally \& in writing with the confidence to engage with a variety of people (clients, colleagues, stakeholders and sub\-contractors alike) and the aptitude to rationally explain, explore and discuss technical issues and capture feedback * Strong organisational skills and attention to detail. Ability to structure \& prioritise work effectively through close cooperation with the team as a whole * Self\-motivation, enthusiasm, hardworking, with ambition and drive * Excellent research and report writing skills. * Ability to work as part of a team, collaborating with colleagues, client and stakeholders as appropriate as well as independently without losing initiative \& focus. * Ability to work flexibly under pressure, responding to changing project and programme demands. * Commitment to continued education and improvement through our approved training schemes * Willing and able to visit other locations on company business * A full driving licence would be beneficial as the role will involve travel for site surveys with equipment and limited public transport access * Previous experience in the ecology sector (eg, with an NGO as a volunteer or within a professional consultancy) would be beneficial, including work placements or relevant voluntary experience. * Good knowledge of Irish environmental law and an understanding of the relevant planning systems **Preferred Qualifications:** * Postgraduate qualification in relevant subject * Proven experience of Phase 1 habitat or UKHab Surveys, Fossitt habitat, IVC and botanical surveys * Working towards obtaining protected species licences, such as bats, smooth newt etc * Membership of or working towards full membership of appropriate professional institution * Evidence of published work in ecology field We celebrate diversity, including neurodiversity, and believe it enriches our team. We welcome applications from all backgrounds and abilities. If you are an applicant with a disability that requires reasonable accommodation to complete the application process, please contact us at ReasonableAccommodations@aecom.com At AECOM, we value everyone's unique contributions and perspectives. If you meet some of the requirements above or have transferable skills you believe would benefit us, we would be delighted to hear from you! **Additional Information** Applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible since CVs will be reviewed, and candidates selected for an advanced offer start. Please include a covering letter with your application and provide all predicted and/or obtained academic grades. You must have permanent right to work in Ireland as we are unable to offer visa sponsorship for this position. At AECOM, we are committed to maintaining a secure and trustworthy recruitment process and take any fraudulent hiring activity seriously. ***To support this commitment, all newly hired employees are required to attend an in\-person Day 1 onboarding at an AECOM office location as a condition of employment.*** **About AECOM** AECOM is the world’s trusted infrastructure consulting firm, delivering professional services throughout the project lifecycle – from advisory, planning, design and engineering to program and construction management. On projects spanning transportation, buildings, water, new energy and the environment, our public\- and private\-sector clients trust us to solve their most complex challenges. Our teams are driven by a common purpose to deliver a better world through our unrivaled technical and digital expertise, a culture of equity, diversity and inclusion, and a commitment to environmental, social and governance priorities. AECOM is a Fortune 500 firm and its Professional Services business had revenue of $16\.1 billion in fiscal year 2025\. See how we are delivering sustainable legacies for generations to come at aecom.com and @AECOM. **Freedom to Grow in a World of Opportunity** You will have the flexibility you need to do your best work with hybrid work options. Whether you’re working from an AECOM office, remote location or at a client site, you will be working in a dynamic environment where your integrity, entrepreneurial spirit and pioneering mindset are championed. You will help us foster a safe and respectful workplace, where we invite everyone to bring their whole selves to work using their unique talents, backgrounds and expertise to create transformational outcomes for our clients. AECOM provides a wide array of compensation, benefits and well\-being programs to meet the diverse needs of our employees and their families. We’re the world’s trusted global infrastructure firm, and we’re in this together – your growth and success are ours too. Join us, and you’ll get all the benefits of being a part of a global, publicly traded firm – access to industry\-leading technology and thinking and transformational work with big impact and work flexibility. As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we believe in each person’s potential, and we’ll help you reach yours. We are a Disability Confident Employer and will offer an interview to applicants who have a disability or long\-term condition, who meet the minimum/essential criteria for the role. Please let us know using this email address ReasonableAccommodationsUKI@aecom.com if you would like to apply through the Disability Confident Interview Scheme. All your information will be kept confidential according to EEO guidelines.

title: Graduate Software Engineer
description: **About UniDoodle** UniDoodle is an Irish EdTech company founded at Maynooth University, pioneering the use of artificial intelligence in education. Our platform uniquely combines AI\-powered grading with handwriting recognition technology \- enabling educators to assess written student responses, not just typed answers. Unlike standard classroom engagement tools, UniDoodle is purpose\-built for exam preparation and academic assessment, serving a specialised and growing market across Ireland and internationally. **The Role** We are seeking a Graduate Software Engineer to join our engineering team at our Dublin office. This is an excellent opportunity for a motivated recent graduate to contribute to cutting\-edge AI and EdTech development, working on technology that is genuinely changing how students learn and how educators teach. **Key Responsibilities** * Contribute to the development of UniDoodle's AI\-powered platform, including handwriting recognition and automated grading features * Write clean, efficient, and well\-documented code across the full stack * Collaborate with product and design teams to deliver meaningful new features * Participate in code reviews and uphold engineering best practices * Identify and resolve bugs and performance issues in a timely manner **Requirements** * A degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related discipline * Solid understanding of web technologies and at least one backend programming language * Interest in AI, machine learning, or EdTech is a strong advantage * Strong problem\-solving skills and attention to detail * Excellent communication and teamwork abilities * A proactive attitude and willingness to take ownership **What We Offer** * A competitive graduate salary (€28,000 – €38,000\) * The opportunity to work on genuinely innovative AI technology with real educational impact * A collaborative, inclusive team culture * Professional development and mentorship from day one * Dublin city\-based role with flexible working arrangements Pay: €538\.00\-€730\.00 per week Work Location: In person

title: HGV Class 1 Driver
description: Are you searching for a long\-term job? Are you hoping to build a career? Driving for Jenkins is an opportunity like none other: We offer permanent and long\-term stability for our drivers, as well as flexibility, to provide our drivers with a work\-life balance. Jenkins Drivers are highly valued members of the business as specialists in what we do and professional representatives of the Jenkins brand as the face of our business transactions. “We at Jenkins are very much aware of the value of our Driver colleagues. We offer long\-term job security in today’s ever\-changing and challenging Transport market. We are leaders in the service we deliver and want to continue to grow this model by investing in, and developing a driving team that are trained, skilled and knowledgeable about our specialist market.” ***\-Paul Harden, Head of Transport*** **What the role does and doesn’t look like…** \-Pre\-loaded trailers \-Most loads offloaded by FLTs \-Non multi\-drop \-Curtain\-sider work \-Allocated driver to vehicle \-Non\-hazardous goods \-Non\-temperature controlled vehicles \-No cage work \-No physically demanding work \-No tail lifts \-No high street offloading \-All offloading/onloading environments set up for Class 1 vehicles \-Newly qualified drivers with no experience are welcome to apply and will be provided full inductions. **Pay Rates** €18\.14 per hour €15 subsistence per day (subject to statutory rules) *Typically our drivers earn in excess of 43k each year* **Don’t just take our word for it, ask our drivers:** “I like the family atmosphere at Jenkins. They are really fair employers\- they listen to my side of the story, I like the way the company is run. I drive a 20\-mile trip to work every day, I’d never travelled that far for work before but it’s worth it. All the kit and equipment is nice. No one else drives my truck\- that means a lot to me. The work’s clean, it’s a nice clean job, and 90% of the loading is indoors. The customer relations are really good, Jenkins always go for quality over quantity. I like it when you go to a customer and they say “Oh thank god it’s Jenkins bringing it\- it’ll be on time.” That’s down to good planning and the work the planning team do. It’s a really good working environment, everything about the job is good; lorries, trailers, people\- it really makes a difference. It’s a great team, I really can’t fault them, we all pitch in. It’s a really pleasant place to work, I get on with everybody. I’ve been here 7 years, it’s the longest I’ve been with any company and I’ve no intention of going anywhere. I still love driving because I’ve got a good job.” ***\-Jenkins Driver*** Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: €43,000\.00\-€47,000\.00 per year Benefits: * On\-site parking Licence/Certification: * C\+E Class 1 Driving licence (required) * CPC qualification (required) Work Location: In person

title: Handler Dublin
description: We have an exciting opportunity for you to join our Operations team as a package handler. **Who We Are** FedEx is the largest express transportation company in the world, connecting more than 220 countries and territories. We work every moment to deliver the most important package of someone’s day. Our team of passionate people know their day\-to\-day work is invaluable in delivering outstanding experience to our customers. 530,000 team members strong, we are as diverse as the world we serve. We love what we do, and we do it well. Our reach is big, and so are our dreams. Join us and let's write our next chapter together. **Working hours** : 39 per week **What You Will Be Doing** * Sorting, scanning, loading, and unloading packages in a fast\-paced environment * Using relevant equipment and technology when needed * Following our Health and Safety regulations * Supporting your supervisor and team members as required **What Do You Bring With You** * Be a great team player * Be able to complete all required training * Be able to communicate effectively, in a fast\-moving environment * Have excellent eye for detail and be able to make quick, accurate decisions **What Do We Offer** * Attractive compensation package * Training to get you started and on\-the\-job learning opportunities * Extensive learning resources to further develop your skills and knowledge * Tuition Assistance Program (\*applicable for FedEx positions with a permanent contract) * Employee Assistance Program for you and your family in difficult life situations * Employee reduced\-rate shipping * Great career opportunities * FedEx is one of the worlds most admired companies and trusted brands year after year Bring your ideas, individuality, and dreams to our global community. Feel good about where you work. Choose your career path \- we’re ready to invest in your development. Join FedEx. Diversity \& Inclusion is more than the workplace. It’s the cultures we celebrate, the dreams we inspire, and creating a global community where everyone is accepted. At FedEx, diversity and inclusion happens because we do it together. FedEx in the UK is Disability Confident ‘Committed’. FedEx was built on a philosophy that puts people first, one we take seriously. We are an equal opportunity employer and we are committed to a diverse and inclusive workforce in which we provide growth opportunities for all Our Company FedEx is one of the world's largest express transportation companies and has consistently been selected as one of the top 10 World’s Most Admired Companies by "Fortune" magazine. Every day FedEx delivers for its customers with transportation and business solutions, serving more than 220 countries and territories around the globe. We can serve this global network due to our outstanding team of FedEx team members, who are tasked with making every FedEx experience outstanding. Our Philosophy The People\-Service\-Profit philosophy (P\-S\-P) describes the principles that govern every FedEx decision, policy or activity. FedEx takes care of our people; they, in turn, deliver the impeccable service demanded by our customers, who reward us with the profitability necessary to secure our future. The essential element in making the People\-Service\-Profit philosophy such a positive force for the company is where we close the circle, and return these profits back into the business, and invest back in our people. Our success in the industry is attributed to our people. Through our P\-S\-P philosophy, we have a work environment that encourages team members to be innovative in delivering the highest possible quality of service to our customers. We care for their well\-being, and value their contributions to the company. Our Culture Our culture is important for many reasons, and we intentionally bring it to life through our behaviors, actions and activities in every part of the world. The FedEx culture and values have been a cornerstone of our success and growth since we began in the early 1970’s. While other companies can copy our systems, infrastructure and processes, our culture makes us unique and is often a differentiating factor as we compete and grow in today’s global marketplace.

title: I.T. Applications Support Analyst, IT & Digital
description: **GET TO KNOW US** At Brown Thomas Arnotts, we are Reinventing Retail. Our purpose is to enrich our customer’s lives, which we achieve by living our values – Going Above and Beyond, Driving Creativity and Innovation, and Doing the Right Thing. We are one business with two iconic brands, bringing exciting experience to life through our digital and physical destinations. Our customer engagement sets the bar in service and experiential retail, giving our customers something to love every time. **KNOW THE ROLE** * This role is responsible for the entire incident management process for all first and second line technical support queries. This will include logging, recording and resolving of incidents * Undertake the initial analysis, resolve yourself or identify correct resolver and monitor until fix validated and deployed * Responsible for tracking all issues and root cause in order to feed into the strategic future direction of the IT systems * It will be of upmost importance that the analyst understands business needs, manages priorities and escalates where required * The role also involves close engagement with stakeholders and suppliers including the internal teams to ensure speedy resolution of all issues * The analyst will participate in regular update meetings with stakeholders communicating updates and reporting back to the business **KNOW WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR** * 1\-2 years Oracle retail (RETEK 10\.1 ) experience advantageous * 1\-2 years Infor WMS (WMS multi\-tenant 2024\.08\.04\) advantageous * Experience with POE (Purchase Order entry) process's * Knowledge of Oracle Retail Integration Bus (RIB) essential * A relevant 3rd Level Qualification (IT, eCommerce, Supply Chain) * 3\-5 Years technical support experience * Experience with SQL would be a significant advantage * Ability to provide 1st and 2nd line support and resolution and provide key details when escalating to internal or partner service desk * Experience managing complex scenarios with multiple partners to ensure the overall business SLA is achieved * Ability to direct, interact and share technical issues * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills with a strong customer focus * Excellent analytical, problem solving skills and attention to detail * Ability to prioritise effectively in order to resolve issues * Ability to manage and analyse complex data * Ability to work under tight deadlines * Ability to adapt to new technology platforms quickly * Demonstrates a flexible \& agile approach to work, with proven examples of this behaviour * Experience in file transfer, encryption technologies * Knowledge of VB.net, Excel VBA * Proven experience in supporting handheld devices **Additional Information:** The location of employment for this role is Brown Thomas Arnotts Head Office, 12 Henry Street, Dublin 1\. The working hours per week are 37\.5 hours and the salary per annum is €40,000\. **KNOW WHAT'S IN IT FOR YOU** We believe that every team member should have the opportunity to curate a meaningful career that enhances professional and personal development. That is why we foster a culture of belonging and inclusiveness where your voice is valuable. A great customer experience starts with a great people experience, and we’re proud to have been recognised by multiple industry bodies for our progressive workplace culture. As a Brown Thomas Arnotts team member, you will benefit from: * Competitive Salary * Contributory Pension Scheme * Competitive annual leave entitlement * Team Member discount across the world’s most iconic brands * Recognition Days for Volunteering \& Wellbeing * Wellbeing Initiatives * Extensive Learning \& Development Offerings * Leading Talent Programmes * Flexible Working Model * Additional Benefits **KNOW HOW WE WORK** **Experience is our business**: Our customer engagement sets the bar in service and experiential retail, giving our customers something to love every time. **We Are More Than Shops:** We create enjoyable places, to excite, where people socialise, discover, learn and are inspired, and where local communities are enriched. **We are Curators:** Our unique range of product and services are key to the sense of excitement that Brown Thomas Arnotts is renowned for, whether for a special day or every day, things that make our customer’s lives better. **We Care for Our People:** We believe a great customer experience starts with a great people experience. That is why we promote a culture where talent is nurtured and where creativity and learning are valued. **We Succeed by Working Sustainably:** We are community\-focused and our aim is to cultivate retail environments that are conscious, considerate and commercial. Brown Thomas Arnotts is an equal opportunity employer committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace where culture and creativity come together. Our future success depends on the perspectives and contributions of all team members – their diverse backgrounds, abilities and experiences make our business stronger. We don’t discriminate on the basis of gender, marital status, family status, age, disability, sexual orientation, race, religion, and / or membership of the Travelling community. We aim to ensure the candidate experience is accessible to all and every new team member who joins Brown Thomas Arnotts can expect to become part of a progressive community where different backgrounds, views \& personalities aren’t just welcomed – they’re positively nurtured. After all, we want you to bring your whole self to work. Please contact us at **careers@brownthomas.ie**torequest accommodation.

title: IT Infrastructure Engineer
description: **Job Description** **Job Title:** IT Infrastructure Engineer **Department:** IT, Infrastructure Engineering Team **Reports to:** IT Manager **Date:** 2026 **Location:** Beacon Hospital **Overall Purpose of the Job** The IT Infrastructure Engineer is responsible for supporting, maintaining, improving, and delivering enterprise infrastructure services across the hospital environment. The role involves a combination of operational support, IT infrastructure project delivery, troubleshooting, and continuous improvement across networking, virtualisation, systems, security, storage, backup, and cloud\-integrated services. The IT Infrastructure Engineer works closely with internal IT teams, including Cyber Security, Helpdesk, and Development teams, as well as clinical departments, vendors, and third\-party providers, to ensure stable, secure, and resilient infrastructure services within a complex healthcare environment supporting 24x7x365 operations. The successful candidate will be a strong IT infrastructure generalist with solid technical fundamentals, strong troubleshooting abilities, and practical experience across enterprise infrastructure technologies, with deeper technical capability in one or more core areas such as networking, virtualisation, systems administration, storage, or security technologies. Engineers are encouraged to develop broad infrastructure knowledge while building deeper technical expertise in one or more infrastructure domains. **Key Responsibilities and Deliverables** **Infrastructure Operations \& Engineering** * Support, maintain, troubleshoot, and improve enterprise infrastructure systems and services across the hospital environment. * Investigate and resolve infrastructure incidents, operational issues, and escalated technical support requests in a timely manner. * Take ownership of infrastructure issues, operational improvements, and assigned technical projects from investigation through to resolution and delivery. * Support and improve enterprise infrastructure platforms, including networking, virtualisation, storage, backup, Microsoft infrastructure, and cloud\-integrated services. * Maintain and troubleshoot enterprise networking environments, including switching, routing, VLANs, VPN connectivity, wireless infrastructure, load balancing, and firewall technologies. * Support virtualisation and server platforms, including Windows, Linux, physical servers, and virtual infrastructure technologies. * Support enterprise storage and backup platforms and participate in backup validation, recovery testing, and disaster recovery activities. * Support identity and infrastructure services, including Active Directory, DNS, DHCP, Microsoft 365, and related infrastructure technologies. * Support infrastructure connectivity and integrations for healthcare and clinical systems. * Support enterprise monitoring, alerting, reporting, and operational stability initiatives. * Maintain technical documentation, operational procedures, implementation notes, and infrastructure diagrams. **Security \& Resilience** * Assist with implementing infrastructure security improvements, including hardening, segmentation, patching, resilience, and access control initiatives. * Support secure infrastructure operations through firewall administration, certificate management, and security best practices. * Participate in resilience and recoverability initiatives across the hospital infrastructure environment. **Projects \& Continuous Improvement** * Participate in infrastructure projects, including upgrades, migrations, expansions, implementations, and service improvements. * Work collaboratively with vendors and third\-party providers during implementations, upgrades, troubleshooting, and maintenance activities. * Contribute to infrastructure standardisation, operational improvements, and continuous service enhancement initiatives. * Work collaboratively across IT teams to ensure stable, secure, and efficient infrastructure operations. * Participate in planned maintenance activities and out\-of\-hours support where required. **Qualifications \& Experience** * Bachelor's degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, Infrastructure, Networking, or related discipline. * Minimum 4\-5 years' experience in enterprise infrastructure, systems, network, or infrastructure engineering environments. * Experience supporting enterprise infrastructure technologies, including networking, virtualisation, Microsoft infrastructure, storage, backup, or security platforms. * Practical experience in one or more core infrastructure areas such as networking, virtualisation, systems administration, storage, or security technologies. * Experience in troubleshooting complex infrastructure and operational issues within enterprise environments. * Experience participating in infrastructure projects, upgrades, migrations, or technology implementations. * Experience working with vendors and third\-party providers during support and project delivery activities. * Healthcare infrastructure experience is desirable but not essential. **Preferred Certifications \& Technical Exposure** * Relevant infrastructure certifications are desirable, particularly in Microsoft, virtualisation, enterprise networking, firewall, cloud, or cybersecurity technologies. * Exposure to enterprise networking, firewall, virtualisation, storage, backup, Microsoft infrastructure, and cloud\-integrated technologies is desirable. * Experience with enterprise healthcare systems and integrations would be advantageous. **Technical \& Operational Competencies** * Strong troubleshooting, analytical, and problem\-solving skills within enterprise infrastructure environments. * Ability to work effectively in a fast\-paced operational healthcare environment supporting critical systems and services. * Ability to manage multiple priorities, operational tasks, and infrastructure projects simultaneously. * Strong communication, collaboration, and stakeholder engagement skills. * Ability to work independently while contributing positively within a team environment. * Strong documentation and organisational skills. * Customer\-focused approach with a strong service delivery mindset. * Willingness to continuously develop technical knowledge and broader infrastructure capabilities. * Ability to participate in planned maintenance activities, on\-call support, and out\-of\-hours operational work where required. **Additional Information** All posts within Beacon Hospital require flexibility to support the delivery of effective and efficient healthcare services. The post holder will be expected to support operational requirements, infrastructure projects, cybersecurity remediation initiatives, and continuous service improvement activities as required by hospital management and the IT department. This job description is intended to outline the areas of responsibility and deliverables at the time of writing. As the Hospital and the post holder develop, this job description may be reviewed in line with changing operational and business requirements.

title: IT Support Engineer
description: ICT Support Engineer \- Long\-Term Public Sector Contract Dublin 2 \| Fully Onsite Day Rate Contract \| Initial 24 Months \+ Strong Extension Potential Looking for a stable, long\-term contract opportunity with broad technical exposure in a public sector environment? We're recruiting an ICT Support Engineer to join a well\-established public sector organisation in Dublin city centre. This role offers the chance to work across end\-user support, Microsoft technologies, endpoint management, and day\-to\-day ICT operations in a structured and collaborative environment. With an initial 24\-month term and strong extension potential, this is an ideal opportunity for someone looking to build contract experience or move into a more stable technical support environment. The Role You'll provide hands\-on ICT support across desktop, endpoint, and user environments while helping to ensure reliable, secure, and responsive IT services. Key Responsibilities: \* Deliver 1st and 2nd line ICT support across hardware, software, and end\-user environments \* Support Microsoft 365 administration, including user management and troubleshooting \* Administer and support Active Directory / Entra ID environments \* Configure, deploy, and support desktops, laptops, and endpoint devices \* Support endpoint management and device life\-cycle activities using Intune and related tools \* Troubleshoot Windows desktop and operating system issues \* Assist with account provisioning, permissions, and access management \* Support networking and connectivity troubleshooting (LAN/WAN, WiFi, VPN) \* Support endpoint security, patching, and compliance activities \* Manage incidents and service requests through ticketing systems \* Escalate and coordinate issues with internal teams and third\-party suppliers \* Maintain technical documentation and contribute to service improvement initiatives Requirements: \* 2\+ years' experience in ICT Support, Desktop Support, Service Desk, or Infrastructure Support environments \* Strong Microsoft 365 administration and support experience \* Experience supporting Windows desktop environments \* Experience with Active Directory and/or Entra ID administration \* Exposure to Intune, endpoint management, or device deployment tools \* Experience with ticketing systems and SLA\-driven environments \* Good understanding of networking fundamentals and troubleshooting methodologies \* Strong customer service and communication skills Desirable: \* Exposure to Azure environments \* ITIL certification or service management exposure \* Public sector, education, or regulated environment experience \* Experience supporting security and patch management processes Why Apply? \* Long\-term contract with strong extension potential \* Highly sought after, stable public sector environment \* Broad exposure across Microsoft and end\-user technologies \* Opportunity to build technical and contract experience \* Dublin city centre location

title: IT Support Engineer L1/L2 - MSP
description: **Level 1 / Level 2 IT Support Engineer – MSP** **South County Dublin \| 5 days office\-based \| MSP experience required** A small, well\-established MSP in South County Dublin is hiring a Level 1 / Level 2 IT Support Engineer to join its service desk team. This role is for someone with around 3\-4\+ years’ IT support experience gained in an MSP environment. MSP experience is required, as you will be supporting multiple clients, managing ticket flow, switching between different environments, and troubleshooting at pace. This is a 5 days per week office\-based role. This is non\-negotiable. You must already be living in South County Dublin, and you must be fully legal to work in Ireland now. There is no visa sponsorship and no relocation support for this role. Most of the work will be service desk based, supporting clients remotely, but there may be occasional site visits when needed. This is not a field engineer role, but you will need to be presentable, client\-focused, and comfortable visiting client sites from time to time. **What you’ll be doing** * Managing and resolving support tickets across multiple clients * Troubleshooting Windows desktop, user, application and connectivity issues * Supporting Microsoft 365, Entra ID / Active Directory and user account issues * Helping users with MFA, Authenticator apps and access\-related problems * Basic network troubleshooting across TCP/IP, DNS and related issues * Supporting security tools and processes such as EDR, MDR and general cyber security hygiene * Communicating clearly with clients by phone, email and ticket updates * Escalating issues properly when needed * Carrying out occasional client site visits when required **What you’ll need** * Around **4\+ years’ IT support experience** * Previous **MSP experience** — this is required * Strong Level 1 support ability, with some Level 2 troubleshooting experience * Good Windows troubleshooting experience * Good understanding of TCP/IP, DNS and basic networking * Experience with Active Directory and/or Entra ID * Exposure to Microsoft 365 * Awareness of MFA, Authenticator apps, EDR/MDR and general cyber security basics * Strong written and verbal communication skills * A positive, can\-do attitude and willingness to keep learning * Good troubleshooting instincts and the ability to move quickly between issues * You must be living in **South County Dublin** * Access to your own car or motorbike would be very useful, but it is not essential This role would suit someone who enjoys the variety of MSP work, wants exposure to different clients and technologies, and is happy working in a small, flexible team. Salary €35,000 \- €39,000\. Apply with your CV if you experience as a Technical Support Engineer and your location fits the requirements.

title: Implementation Consultant – Fintech

The company develops enterprise\-grade platforms used by global clients to manage complex financial operations and reporting processes. The business continues to invest in modern technologies, automation, and AI\-driven solutions to improve efficiency and deliver value to clients. **About the role** We are looking for an **Implementation Consultant** who combines strong technical capability with client\-facing confidence. This role suits someone who enjoys solving complex problems, working with data, and guiding customers through system implementations. You’ll likely have: * **2\-5 years’ experience** in a technical, consulting, or fintech\-related role * Exposure to **financial services, fund accounting, or investment operations** (highly beneficial) * Strong **data handling skills** , with experience in **SQL and/or advanced Excel** * A genuine interest in **AI, automation, and improving operational efficiency** * The ability to communicate clearly with both technical and non\-technical stakeholders * A proactive mindset with strong problem\-solving and organisational skills **Responsibilities** In this position, you’ll work closely with clients and internal teams to deliver and support enterprise software solutions. Your day\-to\-day will include: * Gathering and understanding client requirements and business processes * Configuring and tailoring software solutions to meet customer needs * Supporting system implementations, testing phases, and go\-live activities * Writing and executing test cases and validation processes * Collaborating with development teams to enhance product functionality * Managing and resolving client queries, issues, and change requests * Troubleshooting problems and delivering clear, effective solutions * Supporting integrations, data workflows, and system configurations * Leveraging AI tools and automation to improve efficiency and delivery **Nice to Have / Bonus Skills** While not essential, the following would strengthen your application: * Knowledge of investment products, fund administration, or financial reporting * Experience with SQL, database queries, or data analysis tools * Familiarity with AI tools or modern development assistants (e.g. Claude, Copilot, automation tools) * Exposure to software testing frameworks or development tools * Experience working with support systems or ticketing platforms * Understanding of cloud platforms or modern software delivery environments **Why Join** * Excellent salary package with performance incentives * Great benefits including pension, healthcare, and wellness support * Hybrid working model * Very strong team environment with experienced leadership * Low attrition and long\-tenured team * Clear opportunities for career progression and skill development * Exposure to modern technologies, AI tools, and global clients

title: Information Technology Support Engineer
description: PFH Technology are looking for an experienced **IT Support Engineer** to join our client’s growing team in Dublin. **Objective:** Provide hands on end user support, administration, installation and issue resolution onsite for our client. **Contract:** 12\-month rolling contract **Location** : Dublin **Start Date** : August 2025 **Key Responsibilities:** Responsibilities may include, but are not restricted to: * Ticket management: tickets are assigned by the Helpdesk based offsite, resolve it as per process. * Teams meeting room: Conference room equipment, reactive (may be during a meeting) and proactive testing and updating of meeting rooms check relevant documentation. * Stock Management: Ensure spares are managed for replenishment, good housekeeping for labelling and management of peripherals to ensure adequate tracking. * Prioritise, manage and responding to helpdesk calls in a timely manner logged on the help desk system; * Establish and build on good working relationship with customers * Troubleshoot and if required, referral of hardware faults to third parties * Where appropriate, assign warranties on hardware to third parties * System administration using Active Directory * Troubleshoot issues with Windows accounts/mailboxes * Troubleshoot system problems, diagnose and solve software faults e.g. Citrix * Install and configure computer hardware and peripherals, operating systems and applications * System Administration – amend privileges / security permissions on server shares, setup of new shared folders on existing shares * Manage pst requests and data requests for staff moves * Liaise with third party vendors for software\\hardware installation * Deal with ‘how to’ and information requests including data security and ICT policies * Liaison with local and national ICT teams, e.g. Server and Network management to find root cause for recurring issues. * Document new solutions * Install individual software requests * Managed Print devices – liaise with vendors including IP information * Ability to work alone: responsible for representing PFH on customer site, keeping close with daily PFH team calls (via Teams) raising any potential issues, concerns etc. * Excellent communicator: Highly personable, friendly demeanour. **Required Experience:** * Experience in working on an ITIL aligned Helpdesk * Strong emphasis on managing user profiles, Exchange Online * Experience working in a hybrid AD/Azure AD environment would be an advantage * Strong skills in the O365 suite – with particular emphasis on Outlook/Word/Excel and PowerPoint * Prior experience of MS Teams would be an advantage * Own car * Full Irish Driving Licence essential * MD\-102 certification preferred (held or in progress) **Business Competencies:** * Excellent written and oral communication skills * Excellent teamwork and interpersonal skills * Proven ability to organise work with an organized manner * Self\-starter with a willingness to take responsibility * The ability to interact with key stakeholders in a professional manner **Education:** * Third Level Education Qualification is preferable. title: Information Technology Support Specialist

title: Infrastructure Engineer
description: Crusoe is on a mission *to accelerate the abundance of energy and intelligence* . As the only vertically integrated AI infrastructure company built from the ground up, we own and operate each layer of the stack — from electrons to tokens — to power the world's most ambitious AI workloads. When you join Crusoe, you join a team that is building the future, faster. We're in the midst of the greatest industrial revolution of our time. The demand for AI compute is boundless, and power is a bottleneck. We're solving that — with an energy\-first approach that makes AI infrastructure better for the world and faster for the people innovating with AI. We're looking for problem\-solving, opportunity\-finding teammates with a sense of urgency, who believe in the scale of our ambition and thrive on a path not fully paved — people who want to grow their careers alongside a team of experts across energy, manufacturing, data center construction, and cloud services. If you want to do the most meaningful work of your career, help our customers and partners advance their AI strategies, and be part of a high\-performing team that believes in each other, come build with us at Crusoe. **About the Role:** At Crusoe, the Infrastructure Engineers on our Fleet Operations team play a crucial role in ensuring the reliability and stability of our hardware platform. This role involves both hands\-on diagnosis and repair of rack\-level GPU hardware, as well as developing automation to streamline fleet management, capacity delivery, and maintenance operations. The ideal candidate will be working closely with Data Center Operations and Engineering teams, and playing a key part in the continuous improvement of our hardware platform's reliability and scalability, ensuring that our cutting\-edge infrastructure, featuring the latest NVIDIA and AMD GPUs, continue to operate at peak efficiency for our customers. **What You’ll Be Working On** * Problem Solving and Deep\-Level Troubleshooting: Investigating and troubleshooting problems and hardware faults that our automation can’t determine within our GPU platforms. This will involve taking data from system logs, kernel logs, BMC redfish APIs, and if the data is not there, working with hardware and kernel engineers to add information you need to make accurate determinations. * Coordination and Collaboration: Working closely with our Data Centre Operations, Hardware Engineering and Capacity Planning teams to repair and remediate failed hardware, ensure consistent delivery of new hardware to customers, and roll out new upgrades across the fleet * Automation and Tool Development: Automate routine processes and build Crusoe’s hardware diagnostics, provisioning and repair tooling * Build Processes and Documentation: When you figure out the best way to do something, you’ll be working on building processes, documentation and tooling to help the next person who finds this problem * Validate and Test new hardware: Crusoe is often the first company in the world to get the latest generation AI hardware, before it’s fully tested. Conducting rigorous testing and validation on such cutting\-edge hardware and servers that comes back from repair * On\-Call: Participate in our on\-call rotation, partnering with our US teams to provide follow\-the\-sun coverage **What You’ll Bring To The Team** * Strong analytical, troubleshooting and problem\-solving skills: Our automation takes care of the easy problems, you’ll be digging deep to figure out the hard ones * Linux experience: You’ll have solid unAbout the Rolederstanding of Linux internals and feel at home working in a terminal * Server Hardware and Provisioning: Exposure to server\-class hardware \& provisioning * Fundamentals of Hardware and Networking: You don’t need to be an expert, but you should know if an error message is due to a failed hardware component, a firmware bug, or a networking misconfiguration without escalating * Excellent communication and collaboration skills: You’ll be working with many different people across a lot of different teams – communication is critical * Education: Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science, related field, or self\-educated in computer science fundamentals. **Bonus Points** * Large\-scale GPU operations: We work with cutting edge hardware and software, so we understand most people won’t have worked with it – but it would be nice if you have! * Programming Proficiency: Proficiency with at least one programming language (Python, Go, or similar). **Benefits:** Crusoe also offers a competitive benefits package designed to support financial security, health, and overall well\-being, including pension contributions, private health and dental insurance, income protection, life assurance, and more. **Compensation:** Compensation will be paid as a salary or hourly. Compensation to be determined by the applicant’s education, experience, knowledge, skills, and abilities, as well as internal equity and alignment with market data. Crusoe is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Employment decisions are made without regard to race, color, religion, disability, genetic information, pregnancy, citizenship, marital status, sex/gender, sexual preference/ orientation, gender identity, age, veteran status, national origin, or any other status protected by law or regulation.

title: Infrastructure Engineer

This hands\-on role is responsible for supporting and enhancing core infrastructure, Microsoft 365 services, network operations, and security platforms. The successful candidate will play a key role in delivering secure, reliable, and high\-performing IT services while contributing to infrastructure projects and continuous improvement initiatives. **Key Responsibilities** * Administer and support Microsoft 365, including Exchange Online, Entra ID, and Intune. * Manage Windows and Linux servers, virtualisation platforms, and cloud infrastructure. * Support networking and firewall technologies, including Cisco and Palo Alto solutions. * Troubleshoot complex infrastructure, network, and Microsoft 365 issues. * Deliver infrastructure projects from planning through implementation. * Coordinate with external service providers and support partners to ensure effective service delivery. * Maintain security controls through monitoring, vulnerability management, and patching. * Conduct Disaster Recovery (DR) testing and maintain technical documentation. * Contribute to ongoing service improvements and operational excellence. **Qualifications \& Experience** * Third\-level qualification in Information Technology or a related discipline. * Minimum 5 years' experience in infrastructure engineering, systems engineering, or IT operations. * Strong Microsoft 365 administration and systems engineering experience. * Good understanding of networking, firewall technologies, cloud platforms, and security best practices. * Experience working within a regulated environment would be advantageous. **Desirable Certifications** * Azure Administrator Associate (AZ\-104\) * Intune Administrator (MD\-102\) * Cisco CCNA * Palo Alto PCNSA * CompTIA Security\+ * ITIL 4 Foundation **Key Competencies** * Customer Relationship Management * Planning and Organisation * Problem Solving and Decision Making * Strong Communication and Interpersonal Skills * Collaboration and Teamwork * Drive for Results **How to Apply** To be considered for this opportunity, please submit your latest CV.

Our culture is grounded in our core values and our very strong commitment to delivering every day for our clients and their customers, getting everyone home safely, and caring about your wellbeing. **What we offer:** * Weekly pay based on previous weeks completed work * Weeks 1\-4 Training rate of €14\.15 per hour * Week 5 to end of month 5 \- Field training rate of €14\.42 per hour * Rate card payable from week 5 * Avg earnings on rate card at month 6 is €39k per annum (5 day week Mon\-Sat) * Avg earnings on rate card at month 10 is €47k per annum (5 day week Mon\-Sat) * Double pay on Sunday's **Responsibilities:** * Installing telecommunication cable and equipment as per training * Deliver an excellent customer experience from initial phone call to job completion * Using field apps to report back on activity and works completed * Upskill and attending various training courses * Take due care to prevent loss or damage when working on a customer’s property * Timely accident reporting * Track faults using devices and tools provided * Using power tools and ladders safely * Maintain a clean and presentable appearance always * Adhere to and maintain compliance with all Health and Safety procedures **Requirements:** * A full manual driver’s license is required * Full training provided * Experience using hand and power tools * Manual dexterity and problem\-solving skills * Excellent communication skills required * Time Management * Problem solving skills * KPI focused and willing to work flexible hours to meet the needs of the business **Benefits:** * Pension plan * Life assurance – 4 times your annual salary * Employee assistance programme * Refer a friend scheme * Maternity pay * Educational assistance * Career progression opportunities * Long Service Awards

title: Intern, Student Operations
description: **ABOUT GREYSTAR** Greystar is a leading, fully integrated global real estate platform offering expertise in property management, investment management, development, and construction services in institutional\-quality rental housing. Headquartered in Charleston, South Carolina, Greystar manages and operates over $300 billion of real estate in more than 265 markets globally with offices throughout North America, Europe, South America, and the Asia\-Pacific region. Greystar is the largest operator of apartments in the United States, managing over one million units/beds globally. Across its platforms, Greystar has nearly $79 billion of assets under management, including over $35 billion of development assets and over $36\.5 billion of regulatory assets under management. Greystar was founded by Bob Faith in 1993 to become a provider of world\-class service in the rental residential real estate business. To learn more, visit www.greystar.com. **JOB DESCRIPTION SUMMARY** As a Student Operations Intern, you will support the full lifecycle of a Greystar student community. This role blends on\-site resident hospitality with operational and commercial management, focusing on the unique needs of the student demographic and the critical Summer period. Please note that this is a 10\-week internship during June and August.**JOB DESCRIPTION** ### **Key Responsibilities** * **Full Lifecycle Support:** Own the administrative journey from initial enquiry and room viewings to contract signing, guarantor verification, and the high\-intensity "Summer Turn" periods. * **Front\-of\-House Query Management:** Act as the first point of contact for all resident enquiries, resolving issues ranging from parcel collection and maintenance requests to account queries with speed and empathy. * **Leasing \& Compliance Admin:** Maintain accurate resident records within property management systems ensuring all tenancy documentation and right\-to\-rent checks meet compliance standards. * **Summer Short\-Stay Operations:** Support the transition to short\-term tenancies during the summer months, managing high\-frequency check\-ins for interns and summer school groups to maximize asset occupancy. * **Head Office Operational Support:** Bridge the gap between site and Head Office by assisting the Operations team with portfolio\-wide data entry, occupancy reporting, and process improvement projects. * **Operational \& Financial Admin:** Support lease processing, guarantor verification, and rent collection. Gain exposure to reporting, financial and compliance standards (Maintenance/Facilities/Health \& Safety) to ensure asset protection. ### **About You** * **The Student Expert:** Currently enrolled in a degree (Real Estate, Hospitality, or Business) with an interest in the Living sector. * **Agile Communicator:** Comfortable pivoting from supporting a student to presenting data to an Operations Manager * **Tech\-Fluent:** Proficient in Microsoft Office (Excel is essential) and ready to learn specialised platforms like Yardi or Entrata ***Important Notice:*** *Greystar will never request your banking details or other sensitive personal information during the interview process. Greystar does not conduct any interviews via text or messaging, and all communication will come from official Greystar email addresses (@greystar.com). If you receive suspicious requests, please report them immediately to AskHR@greystar.com.*

title: Investment Analyst
description: ***On behalf of an established Irish real estate investment and asset management business in Dublin, we are looking to hire an Investment Analyst.*** This is a fantastic opportunity for an ambitious, technically strong analyst to join a high\-quality team and develop their career at the heart of a growing real estate investment business. title: Junior Associate - Employment
description: **The opportunity** We are looking for a junior Associate to join our growing team in Dublin.
