title: Junior Implementation Consultant - Financial Services (Need strong Maths)
description: **Junior Implementation Consultant – International Financial Software Company** **Location: Dublin 2** **About the Company** An international software company providing investment administration and regulatory solutions to clients across the UK, Ireland, and the US. Their software supports the management of over €200 billion in assets and is trusted by major financial services providers and outsourcing firms. **About the Role** This is a great opportunity for a junior consultant to join a fast\-growing software company working with global clients. As an Implementation Consultant, you’ll work closely with clients to deliver software solutions that improve efficiency and reduce operational risk. You’ll gain hands\-on experience in consulting, project management, software implementation, and business analysis within the investment and pensions sector. **Key Responsibilities** * Support the implementation of our software with new and existing clients * Analyse client business processes and define software requirements * Help design and configure tailored software solutions * Collaborate with the development team to enhance product features * Assist in testing new software functionality and ensuring quality * Carry out data migrations and system upgrades * Provide training and support to users during onboarding and testing phases **What We're Looking For** * A degree with strong **mathematical or analytical content** * 1\-2 \+years commercial experience working in the Financial Services Industry. * A or B1 in Honours Leaving Certificate Maths * **450\+ Leaving Cert points** * Interest in technology and software * Strong communication and problem\-solving skills * Ability to learn quickly and work independently or as part of a team * Full fluency in written and spoken English * An interest in financial markets or experience with project work is a plus, but not essential.

title: Junior Marketing Executive
description: **Junior Marketing Executive – Cabins \& Containers (Full\-Time)** **Join Our Growing Team** **Cabins \& Containers Ireland** are actively seeking a proactive, creative, and results\-driven **Junior Marketing Executive** to help grow our brand, generate leads, and expand our marketing presence across the Irish market. This is an exciting opportunity for someone who can develop and execute marketing strategies while building awareness for our range of modular buildings, cabins, containers, welfare units, accommodation units, and bespoke modular solutions. **Key Responsibilities** * Develop and implement marketing strategies to promote our range of cabins, containers, modular buildings, welfare units, and accommodation solutions. * Build brand awareness through targeted marketing campaigns across digital and traditional channels. * Manage and grow the company's social media presence on LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram, and other relevant platforms. * Create engaging content including brochures, advertisements, case studies, email campaigns, website content, videos, and social media posts. * Research and identify new target markets, and other key sectors. * Source and manage advertising opportunities across industry publications, online platforms, trade directories, and relevant industry sectors * Maintain and update the company website, ensuring content remains current, engaging, and SEO\-optimised. * Support lead generation activities by developing marketing campaigns that drive enquiries and sales opportunities. * Monitor competitors, market trends, and customer behaviour to identify growth opportunities. * Assist in the planning and promotion of exhibitions, trade shows, networking events, and product launches. * Work closely with the sales team to develop marketing materials and support business development initiatives. * Track, analyse, and report on marketing performance, website traffic, social media engagement, and campaign effectiveness. * Develop and maintain a consistent brand image across all customer touchpoints. **Essential Skills \& Experience:** * Marketing qualification or relevant experience. * Strong digital marketing and social media experience. * Knowledge of SEO and content marketing. * Excellent written and verbal communication skills. * Experience creating marketing materials and campaigns. * Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously. * Strong organisational and analytical skills. * Proficiency with Microsoft Office and marketing platforms. **Desirable Skills:** * Experience within construction, modular buildings, property, accommodation, or manufacturing sectors. * Knowledge of Google Analytics and Google Ads. * Experience with Canva, Adobe Creative Suite, or similar design tools. * CRM and email marketing experience. * Photography and video content creation skills. **Apply Now:** If you are a motivated marketing professional with a passion for building brands, generating leads, and driving business growth, we'd love to hear from you. **Send your CV and cover letter to:** Christina@cabinsandcontainers.ie **Cabins \& Containers – Providing Innovative Modular Solutions Across Ireland and Beyond.** Pay: €29,000\.00\-€32,000\.00 per year Work Location: In person

title: Junior Planner
description: **Are you looking to become part of a collaborative team whilst maximising your potential to grow and develop within your career? Apply today.** **The Role.** GMC are currently looking for a **Junior** **Planner** to join our growing team in Dublin. The successful Planner will be responsible for developing, maintaining, and monitoring project programmes across a range of infrastructure and utilities works, ensuring projects are delivered safely, on time, and within budget. Experience working under NEC contracts is highly desirable. **Key Duties and Responsibilities will include but are not limited to:** **Key Responsibilities:** Develop, manage, and update detailed project programmes using Primavera P6 and/or MS Project Work closely with Project Managers, Engineers, and Commercial teams to produce realistic and achievable schedules Monitor project progress, identify risks and delays, and propose mitigation measures Prepare short\-term and look\-ahead programmes Carry out programme impact and delay analysis where required Support change management and compensation events (particularly under NEC contracts) Attend project and progress meetings, providing planning updates and reports Ensure programmes align with construction methodologies, procurement schedules, and resource plans Assist in the preparation of tender programmes and planning inputs for bids Maintain accurate planning records and reporting dashboards **The Candidate: Required Experience and Competencies:** Degree or relevant qualification in Engineering, Construction Management, or a related discipline 1\+ years experience as a Planner within Civils and/or Utilities projects Strong knowledge of construction sequencing and methodologies Proficiency in Primavera P6 and/or MS Project **NEC Contract Experience Is Preferred** Strong analytical, problem\-solving, and communication skills Ability to work collaboratively with multidisciplinary teams Excellent attention to detail and time management skills **Become a part of something bigger.** At the heart of GMC is our people, from engineers through to customer service and we aim to work collaboratively towards our common goals. GMC is an equal opportunity employer, and we aim to recruit and retain like\-minded people from diverse backgrounds with a wide range of expertise. If you require any specific accommodations, we will make every reasonable effort to support you throughout the recruitment and selection process. Please contact us at careers@gmcirl.com. In GMC we foster a work culture that promotes collaboration and learning, building an environment where our people have the capacity to maximise their potential whilst gaining the opportunity to continuously develop. **Our Future.** Since the founding of GMC in 1981 by Gerry McCloskey, GMC has grown to be Ireland's leading Civils and Utilities company and the future for GMC is bright. We have engaged with our staff and other stakeholders to agree and publish our core values. One of our core values is to *"Focus on the Future"* . Our growth to date reflects the confidence that our customers have in our service delivery, in the innovative approach that we bring to how we do our work, and to our culture of safety and quality. We have ambitious plans to develop our footprint in Ireland and overseas but understand that every decision we make contributes to people's quality of life throughout generations, and we don't take that responsibility lightly as we build Ireland's infrastructure of tomorrow, today.

title: Junior Procurement Officer
description: Junior Procurement Officer What we do at Mckeon Group McKeon Group is a long\-established Irish construction and building services contractor, delivering projects across sectors including commercial, healthcare, education and heritage. With over 70 years of experience, the Group offers construction, fit\-out and facilities services to both public and private clients. Hereworks, its technology division, specialises in smart building solutions, audio\-visual systems and full\-stack integration, enhancing user experience and operational efficiency. Together, they provide a comprehensive approach to building and technology projects, combining traditional craftsmanship with modern innovation. What’s the opportunity? We are currently looking for an ambitious, self\-motivated individual to join McKeon Group \& Hereworks as a Junior Procurement Officer. In this role, you’ll play an important part in our operations team, supporting procurement, accounts, ESG, and fleet and tool management. You’ll report directly to the Procurement Manager and work closely with both internal colleagues and external vendors, while managing documentation and procurement schedules. Benefits of joining Mckeon Group * McKeon Group \& Hereworks prides itself on its exceptional and motivated people and you will be joining a professional, warm, welcoming and enthusiastic team. * Pension Scheme * Income Protection and Death in Service scheme * Membership in Employee Assistance Programme * Excellent opportunity to advance your career and progress within the Group * Competitive salary based on experience and qualifications What will you be doing? (Responsibilities) * Procurement: Prepare and issue purchase orders using our procurement software. Build strong relationships with suppliers to ensure timely handling of enquiries. * Document Control: Support the accounts team by ensuring supplier delivery documentation is processed promptly via our procurement system. * Quotations \& Tenders: Issue enquiries to suppliers for materials, plant hire, tools and vehicles. Review quotes and prepare analysis for the Procurement Manager. * Fleet Administrator: Act as the main point of contact for managing the company fleet. * Hilti OnTrack Administrator: Oversee the management of plant and tools using Hilti OnTrack software. * ESG Support: Support the Procurement Manager with the rollout of our ESG initiatives over the next 12–24 months. Assist with implementing software used to measure and report the Group’s carbon footprint. What do you need? * Minimum of 1\-2 years of experience in an administrative role. * Previous experience in a Procurement role is highly advantageous, although experience does not need to be within the Construction or Technology sectors. * Strong verbal and written communication skills. * Proficient in the use of Microsoft Excel. * Knowledge of Construction and/or Technology, products and services is highly advantageous. * Strong interpersonal skills, with the ability to work well in a team and liaise effectively with various departments. * Excellent problem\-solving abilities, proactive, with a can\-do attitude. * Ability to handle sensitive information with the utmost discretion. **Attributes We Value** * Positive, service\-focused attitude * Results\-oriented approach * Excellent written and verbal communication * High attention to detail * Self\-starter who actively looks for ways to improve * Team player who’s flexible and hardworking * Honest, reliable and quick\-thinking * Strong decision\-making and problem\-solving skills * Effective time management and task prioritisation Practicalities **Salary:** DOE **Location:** Head Office (Ashbourne), client sites as required. A Hybrid working option is available after probation. McKeon Group Core Values

title: Junior Quantity Surveyor
description: Due to continued growth, new contracts and an extensive pipeline of work, Ireland’s largest Civil Contractor have an exciting opportunity for **Junior** **Quantity Surveyors** to join their team working on a large\-scale civil infrastructure projects in **Dublin.** Candidates will be working on residential and large civil infrastructure projects. **Role:** These include but are not limited to: * Procurement, appointment and management of subcontractors \& suppliers through to final account * Produce accurate progress accounts * Preparation and assessment of interim and final valuations for progress payments * Monitor and report on project costs * Collaborate with project teams to identify and mitigate potential risks and cost overruns * Assist the pre\-construction team in the delivery of tenders * Stay updated on industry innovations, regulations etc **Requirements:** * Bachelor’s degree in Quantity Surveying. * 1 year experience in quantity surveying within the Civil Engineering sector or college placement/ internship * Strong knowledge of construction contracts, cost control, and procurement processes. * Proven experience with NEC contracts is essential. * Proficient in the use of COINS, Primavera P6, MS Project software and MS Office suite. * Excellent analytical and problem\-solving skills. * Strong communication and negotiation abilities. * Ability to work independently and as part of a team. **Salary:** * €40K – €50K neg DOE * Company Vehicle/Vehicle Allowance * Benefits Package * Pension If you are a **Junior Quantity Surveyor** considering a career move, please feel to send your CV to **N.williams@gpcireland.com** or contact **Nathan Williams** on **0861502137** for a confidential discussion. GPC will not send any applicants CVs to clients without a full discussion and prior consent. GPC is a specialist construction recruitment consultancy based in Dublin and has more than 24 years’ experience working in the recruitment of construction professionals within Ireland and Internationally.

title: Junior Recruitment Consultant: Fast-Tracked Progression

title: KITCHEN TEAM MEMBER
description: Exciting news! We're opening up a new location in South County Dublin later this Summer! **We’re opening applications now, but we’ll start contacting candidates from July – so if you don’t hear from us right away, we haven’t forgotten you.** We believe eating well should never mean sacrificing taste. Our kitchen team are at the heart of what we do, passionate about food, obsessed with flavour, and focused on delivering fresh, delicious dishes every day. This is a role for someone who loves being in the kitchen, has great attention to detail, and wants to grow their skills in a fast\-paced environment. **Reporting to:** General Manager **Contract:** Full\-time \& Part\-time available **Pay:** €14\.50–€15\.25 per hour depending on experience and role ****What You’ll Be Doing**** * Food Safety \& Compliance – Follow all food safety protocols and ensure everything we serve meets the highest safety standards. * Hygiene Hero – Keep the kitchen spotless and ensure equipment is always sanitised and safe to use. * Allergen Alert – Understand allergens and help ensure every dish is safely prepared for all customers. * Recipe Rundown – Work with prep sheets and help keep the kitchen running smoothly with accurate, consistent prep. * Flavour Focus – Care deeply about taste and presentation. You know great food when you see (and taste) it. * Process Perfection – Know the menu, ingredients and procedures inside out. Follow our SOPs to keep everything efficient and consistent. * Helping Hand – Support the Kitchen Leader with stock checks, rotation, and keeping an eye on kitchen operations. * All Hands on Deck – Ready to jump in when it’s busy, support the service line and work wherever you’re needed most. ****Who You Are**** * Food Enthusiast – You’re passionate about food and get genuinely excited about fresh, seasonal ingredients. * Curious – You enjoy learning new things, ask questions, and want to get better every day. * Innovative Spirit – You love being part of a team that’s always improving and evolving. * Value\-Driven – You bring a strong work ethic and a positive attitude to every shift. * Love for Hospitality – You enjoy the buzz of a restaurant and take pride in great food and great service. * Team Player – Always ready to help your teammates and support the people around you. ****Why Work at Sprout**** * Great food for you on every shift * Growth opportunities – we love to promote from within * Team discount across all locations * Well\-being and mental health support * Regular team socials and celebrations * A fun, friendly team who genuinely care ****What We’re Looking For**** You don’t need to be a chef, but you do need a passion for food and a commitment to quality. Here’s what we value most: * Love for Food – You care about flavour, freshness, and making food that looks as good as it tastes. * Knife Skills – You know your way around a chopping board (or you’re eager to learn). Perfectly sliced carrots? No problem. * Attention to Detail – From portion sizes to plating, you notice the little things that make a big difference. * Good Level of English – You can follow instructions, communicate with your team, and understand food safety requirements. * Reliable \& Organised – You keep things clean, follow checklists, and stay on top of your prep. * Curious \& Willing to Learn – You’re excited to build your skills and grow with the team. If you’re someone who thrives in a team, loves a bit of buzz, and wants to grow in a company that values people and positivity, we’d love to hear from you.

title: Kitchen Porter
description: You will receive training through our Dalata Academy to help you learn important skills. You will also meet experienced team members who can help you grow. At Dalata, we believe in promoting from within. If you work hard, you can have new opportunities in the future. Join us and be part of a friendly team! **Benefits** * Free employee meals on duty * 50% Off F\&B in all properties * €/£60 employee rate on rooms for all Dalata\-owned hotels * 30% off the Friends \& Family rate * Extra AL day for every year worked (5 days to a maximum of 5 days) * Employee Assistance Programme. * Cycle to Work Scheme/Transport Schemes * Pension access (Ask your HR Manager for local pension information) * Development Opportunities through our Dalata Academy\- support your career journey * Staff Appreciation Initiatives, fun team\-building activities and regular charity events **Your Job** * Assist the Head Chef \& Sous Chef with the overall cleaning \& H\&S of the kitchen. * Take direction from department managers and assist in the monitoring of foodwaste and manageing waste collection. * Maintaining a supply of clean ware/cooking ware \& kitchen utensils at all times * Responsible for the cleanliness of the kitchens and areas associated with the kitchen as directed * Work alongside the chef team to maintain HACCP standards **What You’ll Need** * Previous experience working in a kitchen. * Flexibility in working hours as the role includes mornings/evenings \& weekends * Ability to work as part of a team or on own initiative **About Us** Dalata Hotel Group – We bring *the heart of hospitality* to every hotel we own and operate. We see our growth and expansion plans as a way of spreading our unique way of caring for our guests further around the world, bringing better experiences to more people. Currently, the Dalata difference can be felt at each of our hotels in Ireland, the UK, Germany \& the Netherlands, and we’ll keep expanding this further, focusing on key UK cities, Dublin and Continental Europe. Helping our people to grow and develop their careers is vitally important to us and is one of our highest priorities. We have many exciting and unique internal development programmes, each crafted to make sure our employees get the learning they need to be able to take the next step in their careers. Dalata Hotel Group Plc is an Inclusive Employer. We actively encourage people from all backgrounds and abilities to apply to be part of our team. However, we understand everyone's needs are different; therefore, if you require any accommodations with this application process, please highlight your needs when you are completing your application process.

title: La Mer, Sales Consultant, Full Time, Permanent

That is why we promote a culture where talent is nurtured and where creativity and learning are valued. **We Succeed by Working Sustainably:** We are community\-focused and our aim is to cultivate retail environments that are conscious, considerate and commercial.

title: Legal & Government Policy Analyst (AI Training)
description: **About The Role** AI is increasingly being used to navigate some of the most complex and high\-stakes domains in the world — law, government policy, and regulatory compliance. But getting it right requires human expertise. We're looking for professionals with legal, policy, or government knowledge to help evaluate and improve AI\-generated content in these critical areas. Your insight will directly shape how AI systems reason about Irish and European legislation, regulatory frameworks, and public policy — helping build tools that people and organisations can genuinely trust. * Organization: Alignerr * Type: Hourly Contract * Location: Remote * Commitment: 10–40 hours/week **What You'll Do** * Review AI\-generated legal analyses, policy summaries, and government\-related content for accuracy and completeness * Evaluate the proper use of legal terminology, citations, and regulatory references — including Irish statute law, EU directives, and case law * Identify factual errors, logical flaws, or misleading interpretations in AI outputs * Assess whether AI responses appropriately handle nuance across legislation, case law, compliance, and public policy * Provide clear, structured written feedback that helps improve AI performance on legal and government topics * Work independently and asynchronously — entirely on your own schedule **Who You Are** * Strong understanding of legal concepts, government processes, or public policy — Irish or EU frameworks particularly valued * Excellent reading comprehension and analytical thinking skills * Sharp attention to detail — especially around precise language and factual accuracy * Clear, structured written communication style in English * Comfortable evaluating content across diverse legal and regulatory topics * No prior AI experience required **Nice to Have** * Background in law, political science, public administration, or a related field * Experience with legal research, compliance work, or government operations in Ireland or the EU * Familiarity with GDPR, DPC enforcement, Irish data protection or financial regulation * Paralegal, legal assistant, or policy research experience * Knowledge of Irish constitutional law or the common law tradition **Why Join Us** * Work on cutting\-edge AI projects shaping how AI handles legal and government content * Fully remote and flexible — set your own hours and work when it suits you * Collaborate with top research labs and a global community of subject matter experts * Freelance perks: autonomy, variety, and meaningful impact * Build expertise at the intersection of AI and law — a rapidly growing and influential field * Potential for ongoing work and contract extension

title: Loading Shovel Driver
description: **Job Title: Loading Shovel Operator** **Location:** Dublin Port **Department:** Operations / Bulk Stores **Join Our Stevedoring Team in Dublin** Help us continue delivering an exceptional service to our customers and strengthen our position as a market leader in the industry. **Who We Are** Doyle Shipping Group is Ireland’s largest independent shipping company, providing integrated shipping, logistics, and maritime services through our offices in all major Irish ports. With over 130 years of experience in the industry, we bring an unrivalled level of expertise and reliability to our clients across the country. **Summary of Position** The Loading Shovel Operator will be responsible for the safe and efficient operation of the Company’s loading shovels and other mobile plant equipment. The role includes loading cargo into the Company’s stores, preparing orders for customer deliveries, and maintaining all equipment and operational areas. The operator will also maintain accurate records and carry out additional bulk store\-related tasks as required. **Responsibilities / Principal Duties** * Operate the Company’s loading shovels and other mobile plant to load and unload bulk cargo to and from Company stores. * Prepare and clean stores between shipments and perform other operational tasks as required. * Clean, maintain, and conduct routine checks on all loading shovels and mobile plant equipment. * Maintain the cleanliness and upkeep of stores, yards, and ancillary areas. * Keep accurate records and carry out duties related to quality control, feed hygiene, health and safety, and other compliance programmes, including product temperature checks. * Operate the Company’s weighbridge software as required, supporting weighbridge operators. * Undertake any other duties reasonably required by management and support shipside operations where applicable. **Qualifications and Skills** * Full, clean Category B driving licence, or an equivalent licence valid in Ireland. * Fluent in English. * Proven experience operating large loading shovels and related machinery, including loading trailers with high\-tip buckets. * Excellent verbal and written communication skills. * Knowledge of animal feeds, correct handling methods, storage quality preservation, and identification of potential quality issues. **Hours of Work** The normal hours of work are eight hours per day, worked on a rotating two\-shift cycle as follows: * **07:00 to 15:00** * **13:00 to 21:00** Employees will be required to work either shift in accordance with the roster issued by management. In addition, employees may be required to work overtime, including weekends, to meet operational requirements. **Pay Benefits** * Death\-in\-Service Benefit * On\-site parking * Vision/Eye Care **RATE OF PAY \-** DOE

title: Manual Tester
description: **Manual Tester Initial 12 Month Contract Dublin / Hybrid (1 day per week required onsite) Rate €200 \- 280 per day** My client is looking for an experienced Manual Tester to support software testing activity across a large\-scale enterprise technology environment. This contract role would suit a QA professional with strong manual testing experience, excellent documentation skills, and the ability to work closely with both technical teams and business stakeholders. The Job You will be responsible for reviewing requirements and design documentation, creating test scenarios and acceptance criteria, executing test cases, managing defects, and supporting user and business process testing. The role will involve working across system testing, regression testing, user acceptance testing, conformance testing, and disturbance testing. You will also help maintain traceability between requirements, acceptance criteria, test cases, and defects. **Key Responsibilities** * Review functional and technical design specifications for test readiness * Write clear, detailed, and testable acceptance criteria * Identify and define test data requirements * Create and execute test scenarios covering functional, non\-functional, regression, edge case, conformance, and disturbance testing * Raise, track, and manage defects using issue\-tracking tools * Support business users and stakeholders during UAT and business process testing * Maintain traceability between requirements, acceptance criteria, test cases, and defects * Document test processes, results, and quality records * Contribute to test estimation, planning, and delivery documentation * Review automated tests for coverage, quality, and effectiveness where required * Identify suitable areas for test automation * Mentor junior testers and support continuous improvement within the testing process **Have You:** * 5\+ years' professional experience in software testing or quality assurance * ISTQB certification * Strong manual testing experience across enterprise software environments * Strong understanding of testing principles, standards, and best practice * Experience creating and executing detailed test cases and test scenarios * Experience with system testing, UAT, regression testing, conformance testing, and defect management * Strong experience using issue\-tracking and test management tools * Good understanding of acceptance criteria, test data, requirements traceability, and quality documentation * Familiarity with API testing concepts and tools such as Postman or SOAP UI * Experience testing applications built on Java / Angular technology stacks * Working knowledge of SQL, XML, JSON, and Unix/Linux environments * Experience working within Agile, Waterfall, or hybrid delivery methodologies * Strong written and verbal communication skills * Ability to work effectively with technical teams, business users, and wider project stakeholders **Nice to Have** * Awareness of test automation approaches * Experience reviewing automated test coverage * Experience mentoring junior testers * Exposure to large\-scale public sector, financial services, or enterprise delivery environments **Desired Skills and Experience** Manual Tester Initial 12 Month Contract Dublin / Hybrid (1 day per week required onsite) Rate €200 \- 280 per day

title: Microbiology Analyst
description: **Eurofins Scientific is an international life sciences company, providing a unique range of analytical testing services to clients across multiple industries, to make life and our environment safer, healthier and more sustainable. From the food you eat, to the water you drink, to the medicines you rely on, Eurofins laboratories work with the biggest companies in the world to ensure the products they supply are safe, their ingredients are authentic, and labelling is accurate.** **Eurofins is dedicated to delivering testing services that contribute to the health and safety of society and the planet, and to its corporate responsibility to protect the environment and ensure diversity, equity, and inclusion across the entire network of Eurofins companies.** **Eurofins PSS Insourcing Solutions® is a global, award\-winning insourcing solution. We place our skilled team directly at the client site, managing operations seamlessly and eliminating complexities. We ensure a strategic, long\-term service commitment that delivers value directly at the client’ site, utilising their facilities, equipment, and methodologies, while maintaining the same service, expertise, and cGMP compliance available at the Eurofins facility. If you're an employee seeking an exceptional career journey, Eurofins PSS Insourcing Solutions® is your gateway to a future where innovation, collaboration, and success converge. Join us in a world where excellence knows no boundaries!** **We’re proud to be an armed forces\-friendly place to work and welcome applications from reservists, armed forces veterans (including the wounded, injured and sick), as well as cadet instructors, and military spouses and partners.** **If you want to continue to help make our world a safer and healthier place, then apply for the role below and become a part of the extraordinary Eurofins network.** **Job Description** **Unfortunately, we are unable to offer visa sponsorship for this role.** **This is a full time, 12 Month Fixed Term Contract position. 39 hours per week, Working Monday \- Thursday 08:00 – 16:30 \& Friday 08:00 – 15:30\.** As a Microbiology Analyst you will work in a cross functional team to support the Microbiology Quality Control testing function, ensuring compliance with cGMP. **Job Responsibilities that you be covering:** * You will perform a broad range of microbiology testing including bioburden, endotoxin, microbial ID, Biological Indicator testing, BacT/ALERT, sterility checks, and growth‑promotion studies, while carrying out routine laboratory sampling, plate reading, data review, and trending. * You will conduct comprehensive environmental monitoring of cleanroom areas, including viable and non‑viable particulate monitoring and water and gas system monitoring, ensuring uniformity, reproducibility, and GMP‑compliant practices. * You will support and lead quality investigations related to deviations, prepare and revise protocols, reports, and SOPs, and review analytical data for GMP compliance with strong documentation accuracy. * You will collaborate closely with the client team, providing daily updates, maintaining strong working relationships, delivering excellent customer service, and ensuring all decisions align with GMP standards and customer expectations. * You will contribute to training, cross‑coverage, and continuous improvement by developing solutions to problems, identifying opportunities to enhance quality and service, updating documentation, and keeping training records current. * You will maintain a clean, safe, and audit‑ready laboratory environment, operate flexibly with shifting priorities, and consistently demonstrate strong organisation, communication, and problem‑solving skills while working both independently and as part of a team. **Qualifications** **What skills, qualifications and experiences you will hold as a Microbiology Analyst:** * You will hold a degree in Microbiology or a related discipline, along with at least one year of experience in a pharmaceutical regulatory environment. * You will bring hands‑on environmental monitoring experience, strong knowledge of cGMP requirements, and the ability to work independently while meeting established timelines. * You will have excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to form strong working relationships and avoid misunderstandings in technical exchanges. * You will have a customer‑focused mindset, demonstrating ownership for speed, quality, and accuracy, along with a passion for quality and high service standards. * You will bring adaptability, resilience under pressure, and the ability to quickly learn new processes, switch priorities, and contribute effectively within a dynamic, growing team. * You will bring strong organisation, accuracy, and documentation skills, along with the capability to solve problems, work with limited supervision, and support continuous improvement. ****Additional Information**** At Eurofins we are growing, innovating and always learning. We celebrate the achievements of our employees through annual long service awards, recognise our colleagues special life events. **As a Eurofins employee you will benefit from:** * We offer exceptional opportunities for career growth, within a friendly and supportive team. As well as a competitive salary, pension and associated benefits you will enjoy an unrivalled working environment, including an active social calendar and subsidised canteen, all while working in a start of the art client facility\- a leading biotechnology company * Fantastic developmental opportunities and career progression pathways such as leadership, training, compliance and Subject Media Expert. * Full Benefits Package including:\- + Pension + Private Medical Insurance + Income protection and life assurance + Annual discretionary bonus + Birthday Holiday entitlement * Exciting Eurofins Incentives: Employee Activity Committee (EAC), Equality Driving Excellence (EDE), significant Reward and Recognition programme Opportunity to benefit from Eurofins Operational Development training platform, plus potential support for part\-time training (relevant to the position) * Full use of the canteen facilities and barista on the client site * Free parking onsite and onsite electric charging * Additional annual leave days with continued service * The opportunity to work on life saving products **What Happens Next** Our people are the backbone of what we do, so it's incredibly important we find the right individuals to join us. As a potential new recruit you'll be invited to meet the team in the form of an assessment centre or a staged interview process dependent on the role and it's requirements, this will give you the opportunity to see what working for Eurofins is really like and enable us to get you know your key skills and strengths. **Your data** As part of any recruitment process, the company collects and processes personal data relating to job applicants. The company is committed to being transparent about how it collects and uses that data and to meeting its data protection obligations. We embrace diversity! Eurofins network of companies believe in strength and innovation through diversity, being an Equal Opportunity Employer. We prohibit discrimination against employees or applications based on gender identity and/or expression, race, nationality, age, religion, sexual orientation, disability, and everything else that makes employees of Eurofins companies unique. **For more information about Eurofins PSS® please visit our** **website** **and watch a message below from our Global Head, Beth Di Paolo.**

title: Microsoft Engineer
description: Our client, based in Dublin, is seeking an experienced Microsoft Endpoint \& Identity Engineer to support modern workplace and endpoint management initiatives within a large enterprise environment. This contract role will focus on Microsoft Intune, Microsoft 365, endpoint management, identity services, and modern deployment technologies including Windows Autopilot and Windows Hello for Business. **Key Responsibilities** * Administration and optimisation of Microsoft Intune / Endpoint Manager * Design and support of endpoint enrolment and device lifecycle management processes * Deployment and support of: Windows Autopilot, Windows Hello for Business * Management of device compliance policies, configuration profiles, and application deployments * Integration and administration of Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) services * Support of identity and access management controls including Conditional Access and device\-based access policies * Troubleshooting endpoint management, enrolment, and policy\-related issues * Support modern workplace transformation and security improvement initiatives * Production of technical documentation and operational procedures * Collaboration with internal IT teams and knowledge transfer activities **Required Skills \& Experience** * 5\+ years’ experience in Microsoft infrastructure, endpoint management, or modern workplace engineering roles * Strong hands\-on experience with: * Microsoft Intune / Endpoint Manager * Microsoft 365 administration * Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) * Experience managing endpoint compliance, device policies, and application deployments * Strong troubleshooting capability across endpoint and identity technologies * Experience supporting enterprise Windows environments **Desirable Experience** * End\-to\-end Autopilot deployment experience * Experience implementing Windows Hello for Business * Knowledge of identity governance and access management principles * Experience delivering endpoint modernisation or transformation projects * Familiarity with security\-focused device management practices **How to Apply** To apply, please send your latest CV outlining your relevant experience.

title: Network Engineer
description: **Job requirements:\-** * **Required Qualifications** * Education: Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Information Technology, Network Engineering, or a related field. * ·Experience: Proven hands\-on network engineering / administration experience, **15\+ years** * **Certifications (Desired):** * General: CompTIA Network\+ or Security\+ * Cloud: Cloud certifications beneficial (CCSK and/or an AWS certification) * **Technical Skills:\-** * · Deep understanding of networking protocols (TCP/IP, BGP, OSPF, DNS, DHCP, MPLS, IPsec). · Hands\-on experience with enterprise\-grade equipment from vendors like Cisco, Aruba, and Palo Alto. * · Proficiency with network monitoring and diagnostic tools (e.g., Wireshark, SolarWinds). * · Knowledge of cloud networking and virtualization technologies. * · Familiarity with scripting and automation tools/languages (e.g., Python, Bash) is increasingly beneficial. * · Ability to follow precise company standards in design and configuration of new networks. * Soft Skills * · Strong analytical and complex problem\-solving abilities. * · Excellent written and verbal communication skills to explain technical issues to non\-technical stakeholders and also to clearly communicate with people with varying skills in English. * · Time management and project management skills to prioritize tasks and meet deadlines. * · Ability to work independently and as part of a collaborative team. * · Excellent attention to small details, both technical and logistical and differences between all global locations. * · Ability to work globally, sensitively and across many cultures and countries.

title: OEM Business & Market Development – Drive Products
description: At **ABB**, we help industries run leaner and cleaner—and every person here makes that happen. You’ll be empowered to lead, supported to grow, and proud of the impact we create together. Join us and help run what runs the world. **This position reports to:** Local Division Manager \_\_ In this role you will be responsible for driving the sales strategy and G2M model for sales in the Drive Products and IEC LV Induction motors market within Irelands OEM (Original Equipment Manufacturers), machine building and industrial market sector, aligned with the division strategy. This includes low voltage drives, PLC \& HMI, and IEC low voltage motors offering to different types of OEMs and machine builders across different segments and applications within the Irish and Northern Irish market. Delivers both qualitative and quantitative sales targets to ensure sustainable order growth, revenues, profitability, market share, and customer satisfaction. Responsible to identify and developing new OEM sales opportunities. **Our Team Dynamics** Our teams support each other, collaborate, and never stop learning. Everyone brings something unique, and together we push ideas forward to solve real problems. Being part of our team means your work matters \- because the progress we make here creates real impact out there. **What’s in it for you?** We want you to bring your full self to work—your ideas, your energy, your ambition. You’ll have the tools and freedom to grow your skills, shape your path, and take on challenges that matter. Here, your work creates impact you can see and feel, every day. **Your Role and Responsibilities** In this role, you’ll help run what runs the world, by taking on meaningful work that drives real impact. * *Work model: Office based with travel and flexibility.* * Review the local market, identify and execute predefined market strategies. * Drive volume, mix, and profitability targets for selected customers across the division. * Build and maintain strategic customer relationships to understand their needs and position ABB's drive product solutions. * Leverage cross\-division synergies to enhance customer value and ensure a seamless sales experience. * Develop short\-to\-medium term sales plans using ABB tools. * Conduct regular pipeline reviews and propose recovery actions for any shortfalls. * Prepare competitive offers in coordination with the bid and proposal team, ensuring technical and financial accuracy. * Validate pricing, trading conditions, and margins on all proposals. * Communicate delivery timelines, payment terms, and contractual conditions aligned with ABB strategy. * Develop and communicate value propositions through marketing activities. * Manage sales administration procedures, commercial decisions, and support project management as needed. **Qualifications for the Role** * Degree in Automation, Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, or related field * Marketing / Business qualification or some time working in a similar business environment with a track record of building customer relationships considered a bonus * Previous experience in engaging business to position ABB as a valued solutions provider vrs competitors * Results\-oriented mindset combined with the engineering and business acumen * Strategic in approach and confidence to take initiative, and turn ideas into action * Strong communication and collaboration skills with the ability to build trust across stakeholders * Genuine drive to learn, grow, and continuously improve, both personally and as a sales individual **Benefits** ABB offers a full range of benefits to help you thrive at work and beyond. * Competitive Salary * Private Medical Allowance for Yourself and Family * Competitive Contributory Pension Scheme * Employee Share Acquisition Plan * Internal Mobility Opportunities, Mentorships \& Access to Projects Globally * Employee Assistance Programme * Generous Employee Referral Scheme * 25 Days Annual Leave Plus Bank Holidays * Company Car * Flexible Working * Generous Bonus Scheme **More about us** ABB's Drive Products and IEC Low Voltage Motors division delivers motors, integrated motors, drives, and automation solutions for OEMs and machine builders globally. Our comprehensive portfolio combines ultra premium\-efficiency IEC Low Voltage Motors, variable frequency drives, AC500 PLCs, and HMIs to create optimised automation packages that reduce costs, reduce downtime, improve process throughput, and lower operational carbon footprint. With electric motors consuming 45% of global electricity and high\-efficiency systems cutting consumption by 10%, our integrated solutions help OEMs across blue\-chip manufacturing, water, food \& beverage, mining, and data centers build smarter, more efficient machines and operations. Join us. Be part of the team where progress happens, industries transform, and your work shapes the world. Run What Runs the World. \#ABBCareers Building a cleaner, smarter future takes all kinds of minds: the curious, the courageous, and the creative. That's why we welcome people from all backgrounds and experiences. Ready to make an impact? Apply today or visit **https://www.abb.com** to learn more about the impact of our solutions across the globe.

title: Online Fulfilment Assistant
description: We are looking for individuals to join our exciting Boots.ie order picking team in\-store. You will work in a Boots store and report into the Assistant or Store Manager and your role will be to pick and pack customer orders, work in our back shop, run our stock room and support with deliveries and stock management. **About The Role** To support our Boots.ie operation we have implemented several programs nationally in\-store to ensure we meet the demand of our customer’s needs. As part of this role, you will: * Pick and pack online customer orders to a high standard using a handheld device * Obtain the relevant information required for picking, packing \& dispatching of orders. * Locate all the required goods in the picking areas across the store. * Use the Pod equipment safely and effectively * Comply with the organisation’s policies and procedures and all relevant legal, safety and operating requirements relating to picking, packing and dispatching of online orders * Support the wider store operational activities **What You’ll Need To Have** Full training is provided for this role and these are the essential skills or experience needed to succeed in this role: * Understanding of an online pick/pack operation * Enjoy following process and working at pace * A problem solving mindset * Enjoy working collaboratively as part of a team. * Able to communicate and listen effectively. * Digitally savvy and comfortable embracing new technology, including handheld mobile devices to process online orders * A passion for providing great customer care \- while most of your work will happen behind the scenes, you may occasionally support out on the shop floor working with customers. * A flexible approach to working hours and patterns **It would be great if you also have** These are desirable skills or experience and are not essential, so we would welcome applications from candidates that don’t match this additional criteria. * Experience in a retail or warehouse operations environment * Experience with inventory systems management * Familiar with handheld Pods and IT systems **Our Benefits** * Pension membership (PRSA) * Partial paid maternity leave * Discretionary annual bonus * Generous employee discounts * Flexible benefits scheme We have a great range of benefits in addition to the above that offer flexibility to suit you \- find out more at **boots.jobs/ireland** . Exclusions may apply, eligible roles only. *Please note, any salary estimates given on third\-party sites are not provided or endorsed by Boots and may not be accurate.* **Why Boots** At Boots, we foster a working environment where consideration and inclusivity help everyone to be themselves and reach their full potential. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer, passionate about embracing the diversity of our colleagues and providing a positive and inclusive working environment for all. As the heart of everything we do at Boots, with you, we change for the better. **What's Next** If your application is successful, you'll be invited to an in\-store interview in the next 14 days. Where a role is advertised as full\-time, we are open to discussing part\-time and job share options during the application process. If you require additional support as part of the application and interview process, we are happy to provide **reasonable adjustments** to help you to be at your best.

title: Operations & Outsourcing Co-Ordinator (12 month Fixed Term Contract)
description: Fineco Asset Management DAC (‘Fineco AM’) is a Dublin based Asset Management Company, and asubsidiary of FinecoBank S.p.A. (“FinecoBank”). FinecoBank (“Fineco”) is a leading European bank with a 20\-year history and a fully digital DNA with a branchless approach since the start. Fineco is one of the banks with the widest products range available. Fineco have developed a truly one\-stop\-solution which includes trading, investment and payment services. Fineco have developed a 100% proprietary platform for trading and investment which covers all customer needs from trading listed and OTC product to investment product such as mutual funds and ETF. Fineco AM, which started its operations in May 2018, is authorized by the Central Bank of Ireland as a UCITS \& AIFM Management Company with a MIFID top up, and it currently has approximately €39bn in assets under management.Fineco AM is a competence centre\- specialised in the management of fund\-of\-funds, single strategy funds, as well as Structured Solutions Funds. In addition to portfolio management activities, all key supporting and controlling functions, such as Compliance, Risk, Finance and Legal, are also performed locally. The company started with 5 employees and has experienced strong and consistent growth, with a current headcount of approximately 90 FTE’s. Currently Fineco AM manages 3 UCITS Umbrella Funds – 1 in Luxembourg and 2 in Ireland **Main Responsibilities:** We are looking to recruit enthusiastic professionals, reporting to the Head of Operations to support our UCITS Management Company business. * Be the first point of contact with the investment managers, in relation to outstanding issues as they arise, including settlement, invoicing of the funds and cash flows; * Be the first point of contact with custodian and the fund administrator, in relation to outstanding matters regarding settlement and dividend distribution and any other corporate actions; * Liaise with custodian(s) to open new accounts on behalf of sub\-funds, sub\-Advisors, etc. * Liaise with tax advisors to ensure proper and timely WHT filing/CGT rate applied and rebates reclaimed. * Support overall delegate/outsourced service oversight programme in accordance with company’s internal policies and procedures * Manage the end\-to\-end operational set\-up requirements for static data, notifications, filings, etc. for new fund launches * Collect, review and validate fund expense fees including administration, custody, other fund service providers whilst ensuring they are in line with our funds’ General Expense Budgets; manage communications with investment managers and service providers to ensure timely and accurate payment * Contribute to the production and maintenance of a General Expenses budget for the funds and review, on a periodic basis accruals in the funds NAVs against invoiced amounts. * Assist in the periodic review of NAVs, EMIR Reporting. Ongoing Charges etc. as produced by the Firm’s delegates. Report, track to resolution any exceptions observed. **Key Requirements:** * Third level qualification in Accountancy, Finance or a Business discipline. * 2\-3 years' experience in Operations/Middle Office/Custody. * Good knowledge of financial products and financial markets. * Very good knowledge of Excel. * Working knowledge of AI tools an advantage. * Excellent verbal and written communication skills. * Strong attention to detail. * Well organized with strong time management skills. * Good team player. * Be enthusiastic and eager to learn, self\-starter. **What we offer:** * **Growth Opportunities:** Unlock your potential with career growth and professional development paths. * **Innovative Team:** Be part of a dynamic and innovative team environment. * **Collaborative Culture:** Thrive in a collaborative company culture that values your contributions. * **Generous Time Off:** Enjoy 25 days of holidays to relax and recharge. * **Hybrid Working:** Benefit from a flexible hybrid working policy, 3 days in the office, 2 days flexibility to be remote. * **Health and Dental:** Comprehensive Healthcare and Dental coverage for your well\-being.

title: Optical Network Engineer (Operations)
description: **Position: Optical Network Engineer (Operations)** **Type: Contract (12 months, strong scope for extension)** **Location: Dublin (onsite)** Optical Network Engineer required to join a team supporting a large\-scale, global optical network. his is very much an operations\-focused role which revolves around day\-to\-day running of the network. **Key Duties:** * Supporting and maintaining DWDM / optical transport networks in a live environment * Monitoring performance and keeping things running smoothly * Troubleshooting issues and responding to incidents * Getting involved in root cause analysis and ongoing improvements * Working closely with other engineering teams **Experience required:** * **Solid hands\-on experience with DWDM / OTN (essential)** * Background in network operations / support / NOC environments * Strong troubleshooting skills in live networks **Nice to have:** * Some scripting/software automation experience (Python, Bash, etc.) * Any exposure to network design * Experience across different vendor If you’ve been working on the operations side of optical networks and are open to a move, feel free to message me or apply. Please note, candidates must be based in Ireland with valid visa to apply for this position. You must also be available to go onsite to the office in Dublin on a Mon\-Fri basis.

title: Order Management Specialist
description: **The opportunity** Reporting to our Market Manager for Ireland, the **Order Management Specialist** will help to shape our customer experience by supporting the smooth and accurate processing of orders, from confirmation through to delivery. This position is based at our Dublin Office in Liffey Valley. This entry‑level position works closely with sales, logistics, supply chain teams, vendors, and customers contributing to the effective management of orders, timely resolution of queries, and the delivery of consistent service standards. You’ll be encouraged to take ownership of your work, learn through experience, and contribute ideas that support the wider team and business. We will provide hands\-on training and development for this role. **How you’ll make An Impact** * Ensure timely release of orders and a fulfilment of pre\-established standards and policies to achieve customer satisfaction. * Monitor and resolve order issues and process errors. * Act as the point of contact for clients with queries, quotes, order processing. * Work with the Supply Chain team on order management and shipping support. * Prepare dispatch and shipping documentation for customers, qualify payments. * Enter orders into SAP and maintain order databases. * Create purchase orders for suppliers and internal work orders. * Work with wider Sales and Project **Your background** * Previous experience in a Customer Service/Administrative/Order Processing environment desired, not essential * A keen eye for attention to detail and accuracy * The ability to communicate effectively, over the phone, via email and face to face * Good PC skills, including Microsoft Office suite, specifically Outlook, Word and Excel desired Publication date : 2026\-06\-15

title: Part Time Seasonal Invigilator
description: **Job Opportunity at IMMA** **Open Competition for the role of** **X8 Part Time Seasonal Invigilator** **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_** **About IMMA** The Irish Museum of Modern Art, based in what was the Royal Hospital Kilmainham, an extraordinary 17th century building, set upon a hilltop of 48 acres overlooking the river Liffey, is a witness to at least a millennium of change. All of which feeds into our contemporary thinking and commitment to celebrate this remarkable site. At IMMA we have begun to create a space unique with contemporary culture. This is a space where local and global connect, a space where contemporary art and contemporary life meet, challenge and inspire one another. **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_** **Role Overview** To work as part of the Visitor Experience Team to deliver a high standard of customer service and create a welcoming, engaging, and safe environment for all visitors and employees, while ensuring the museum, its property, assets, and surrounding areas are maintained securely through vigilance and due diligence. To maintain a professional and approachable manner at all times, delivering excellent customer service by engaging with visitors and providing clear, relevant information on the museum’s galleries, exhibitions, artists, and public programmes. **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_** **Key Responsibilities** *Key responsibilities to be undertaken based on priorities agreed with HOD and subject to the needs of the Department/ Programme.* * Proactively engage with museum guests to deliver an excellent standard of customer service, creating a welcoming, informative, and positive visitor experience for all. * Will safeguard the museum’s property from Theft and Damage at all times and ensure that the museums visitor guidelines are carried out at all times through effective communication and invigilation. * Ensuring that all Galleries and exhibitions are open and ready to admit visitors on time by good time keeping and teamwork. * Ensure all visitors to IMMA are treated with respect and as far as practicable have enjoyed their experience though staff interaction with them. * Will be capable of invigilation and associated duties for exhibitions and events held at the Museum or other locations that may be utilised temporarily for the Irish Museum of Modern Arts programmes. * Will undergo all relevant training, relating to staff and visitor emergency evacuation procedures * Dealing with all visitors in responding positively in providing information and promoting the museum’s current and forthcoming programs. * Promotion of the museum’s patrons and members programs, current publications and forth coming talks and lectures when working in the galleries or Front of House **Communication** * To be capable of communication and interaction with visitors on all levels * To be capable of communication professionally with all colleagues **Programme Evaluation** * Liaising with the departments Supervisors and where practicable with Curators to provide accurate mid exhibition feedback and Post Exhibition feedback of the visitor experience **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_** **Key Performance Indicators:** * Will be capable of assisting in the physical evacuation and subsequent security of the museum in the event of fire evacuation or any other emergency that may affect the operation of the museum. * Will be able to deal with the public and respond positively in providing relevant information on the museum’s current and forthcoming programmes. * Will be a team player and capable of working within the team to achieve the goals that have been set out within the museums strategy statement. * Maintain a proactive contribution to ensuring that IMMA provides a welcoming, informative and accommodating experience for all visitors * A commitment to engaging with the public on the gallery floor with the necessary information and skills to deliver information to the best of their ability. * Proactive invigilation and communicative skills utilised in protecting exhibitions * Will undertake statutory and relevant training as directed by the museum. Including mandatory Child Protection Training * Punctuality and reliability in working as a team player within the Visitor Engagement Team to deliver the department and IMMA’s goals **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_** **The successful candidate will have:** * Excellent communication skills and the ability to work as part of a team. * Experience in a customer focused role. Relative experience in the museum or tourist/hospitality sector an advantage. * Experience with computers to ECDL standard or similar. * Confident in asserting themselves when dealing with emergencies such as fire evacuation. * Empathetic and capable of understanding a range of visitor’s needs. Particularly in more challenging situations. * Experience working as part of a friendly and interpersonal team. * The ability to work under pressure and multi\-task. * An interest in the visual arts is an advantage. **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_** **Why Work with IMMA?** **At IMMA, we are proud to offer:** * Opportunity to get involved in IMMA’s 2024\-2028 Strategy, development of key themes in response to current global cultural concerns which will shape our programming over the next five years. * The opportunity to work in a beautiful serene historic 17th Century Building surrounded by 48 acres of Meadows and Gardens – an oasis in Dublin City. * Free Parking / Secure Bike Sheds. * Career development opportunities through mentoring, coaching, and training. * Government Pension Scheme * Employee Wellbeing Committee. * Employee Assistance Programme. * Generous Public Sector leave benefits. * Strong values\-based and inclusive culture. * Strong commitment to diversity, equity, and inclusion. * Great team environment working to our Core Tenets: Contemporary, Inclusion, Innovation, Collaboration and Globally Connected. * Valuable experience at a leading cultural institution. * Opportunities to attend special museum events and exhibitions. * Networking opportunities with professionals in the museum and cultural sectors. **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_** **Salary** The salary for this role is in line with public sector pay scales, starting at **€36,227**, Point 1 of the Grade **Invigilator** (PPC) scale, as of 1st June 2026\.
